markdown:
<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 12"
    pfs=""
>
[Ring of Stoneshifting](/Equipment.aspx?ID=634)
</title>

<spoilers>May contain spoilers from Extinction Curse</spoilers>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Conjuration" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=33" />
<trait label="Earth" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=55" />
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=98" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
<trait label="Teleportation" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=156" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Pathfinder #153: Life's Long Shadows](/Sources.aspx?ID=29) pg. 69

**Price** 1,800 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** worn

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This simple iron ring is faceted with a bulbous geode lined with bright purple crystals. A _ring of stoneshifting_ grants you the ability to ignore difficult terrain caused by rubble or uneven ground made of stone and earth.

**Activate** <actions string="" /> 1 minute;  **Frequency** three times per day;  **Requirements** You are standing on at least 5 feet of earthen material (such as stone, soil, clay, or sand); **Effect** You sink into the ground and emerge at another location within 100 miles. This location must have an earthen surface at least 5 feet deep and you must be able to identify the location precisely by both its position relative to your starting position and its appearance or other identifying features. You can’t carry [extradimensional](/Traits.aspx?ID=67) spaces with you to the destination, and if you attempt to do so, the activation fails.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Ring of Stoneshifting

markdown:
<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 9"
    pfs="Limited"
>
[Shoony Shovel](/Equipment.aspx?ID=635)
</title>

<spoilers>May contain spoilers from Extinction Curse</spoilers>

<traits>
<trait label="Rare" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=137" />
<trait label="Earth" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=55" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Pathfinder #153: Life's Long Shadows](/Sources.aspx?ID=29) pg. 69

**Price** 600 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 2 hands

**Bulk** 2
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This ornate, compact shovel has a golden handle wrapped with beautiful crimson [bulette](/Monsters.aspx?ID=61) leather. Its silver head never gets dirty or worn no matter how much you use it. Its true magic, however, is in its ability to dig on its own.

**Activate** <actions string="Two Actions" /> command, envision;  **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** You place the shovel in a starting position and specify the dimensions and direction to dig (such as “Dig down 8 feet in a rectangle sized 30 feet by 40 feet” or “Dig straight ahead for 30 feet”). The shovel then animates and digs on its own at a rate of one 5-foot cube per 10 minutes until the task is complete or it is picked up. It can dig through dirt, gravel, sand, snow, or similar loose material, but the shovel stops digging (or digs around, if commanded) if it strikes stone or another solid material. A _shoony shovel_ can sense if its digging path will cause a building to collapse, harm a creature, or otherwise create significant problems, in which case it automatically stops digging.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Shoony Shovel

May contain spoilers from Age of Ashes **Source** [Pathfinder #147: Tomorrow Must Burn](/Sources.aspx?ID=8) pg. 71 **Usage** held in 1 hand **Bulk** L --- A [_dreamstone_](/Equipment.aspx?ID=507) can become cursed if left exposed to creatures that corrupt sleep, generate nightmares, or otherwise prey on sleeping or dreaming victims via supernatural methods. A _cursed dreamstone_ seems to function as a normal _dreamstone_ until the bearer falls asleep or is forced to attempt a saving throw against a sleep effect. At this point, the person carrying the cursed _dreamstone_ must attempt a DC 26 Will save to resist the curse’s effects. **Critical Success** The character resists the curse entirely and experiences a vivid dream while sleeping that warns them of the nature of the _cursed dreamstone_.
**Success** The character resists the curse.
**Failure** As long as the cursed character possesses the stone, they are fatigued whenever they wake from sleep, whether natural or magically induced, and they take a –2 item penalty to all saving throws against sleep effects instead of gaining the normal bonus from a _dreamstone_.
**Critical Failure** As failure, and whenever the bearer attempts a saving throw against a sleep effect, they get the outcome that is one degree worse than the result of their saving throw.
name: Cursed Dreamstone

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 13"
    pfs="Limited"
>
[Ankylostar](/Equipment.aspx?ID=644)
</title>

<spoilers>May contain spoilers from Extinction Curse</spoilers>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
<trait label="Transmutation" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=157" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Pathfinder #154: Siege of the Dinosaurs](/Sources.aspx?ID=31) pg. 75

**Price** 2,800 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

**Bulk** 1
</row>

<row gap="medium">
**Base Weapon** [Morningstar](/Weapons.aspx?ID=8)
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This hefty spiked club is made from the fossilized tail-club of a young ankylosaurus. It can be wielded as a _+2 greater striking [morningstar](/Weapons.aspx?ID=8)_.

**Activate** <actions string="Single Action" /> command;     **Effect** The _ankylostar_ grows and transforms into a _+2 greater striking greatclub_, but it retains the [versatile (piercing)](/Traits.aspx?ID=200) trait of a morningstar. While in this form, the _ankylostar_ has Bulk 2 and requires two hands to wield. It can be returned to its morningstar form with another 1-action command.

**Activate** <actions string="Two Actions" /> command, Interact;  **Frequency** once per day;  **Requirements** The _ankylostar_ is in its greatclub form; **Effect** The _ankylostar’s_ length extends as you swing the club in a wide arc. You Strike up to three creatures in a 15-foot cone; this counts as one attack for the purpose of calculating your multiple attack penalty. Alternatively, you [Shove](/Actions.aspx?ID=38) up to three creatures in a 15-foot cone, rolling a separate [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) check for each creature.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Ankylostar

May contain spoilers from Extinction Curse **Source** [Pathfinder #154: Siege of the Dinosaurs](/Sources.aspx?ID=31) pg. 75 **Price** 1,650 gp **Usage** held in 1 hand **Bulk** 1 **Base Weapon** [Shield Spikes](/Weapons.aspx?ID=42) --- This heavy bone shield is composed of a massive plate of dinosaur bone with jagged, tooth-like ridges ringing its edge. It has Hardness 12, HP 48, and BT 24. The ridges are unremovable _+1 striking [shield spikes](/Weapons.aspx?ID=42)_ that deal slashing damage instead of piercing damage. While holding the _jawbreaker shield_, you gain a +3 item bonus to your Reflex DC to resist [Swallow Whole](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=35) attacks. **Activate** Interact; **Trigger** A creature attacks you with a jaws or fangs Strike or similar Strike using its mouth; **Effect** You make a shield bash Strike as a reaction against the attacker. If the shield bash hits, the target takes damage and must attempt a DC 31 Fortitude saving throw. The effects of the saving throw are determined after the effects of the triggering Strike are resolved. **Success** The target is unaffected.
**Failure** You wound the target’s mouth. It takes 1d6 [persistent bleed damage](/Conditions.aspx?ID=29) and takes a –2 status penalty to attack and damage rolls with jaws, fangs, or similar Strikes for 24 hours.
**Critical Failure** You break the target’s jaw. As failure, and the creature can't make Strikes using its mouth for 1 minute. At the end of its turn, it can attempt a Fortitude save to regain the ability to make Strikes using its mouth.
name: Jawbreaker Shield

When worn, it infuses your body with a ghastly and nauseating flavor. **Activate** envision; **Trigger** You are grabbed or are hit by an unarmed attack; **Effect** Your attacker must attempt a DC 28 Fortitude saving throw. Creatures with a stench aura, such as [otyughs](/Monsters.aspx?ID=327) and xulgaths, are immune to this effect. **Success** The creature is unaffected and is immune to the effects of the noxious jerkin for 1 minute.
**Failure** The creature is [sickened 1.](/Conditions.aspx?ID=34)
**Critical Failure** The creature is sickened 1 and is [slowed 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=35) for 1 round.
name: Noxious Jerkin

May contain spoilers from Extinction Curse **Source** [Pathfinder #154: Siege of the Dinosaurs](/Sources.aspx?ID=31) pg. 76 **Price** 1,200 gp **Usage** held in 1 hand **Bulk** L **Base Weapon** [Javelin](/Weapons.aspx?ID=71) --- This stone-headed _+2 striking [returning](/Equipment.aspx?ID=301) [javelin](/Weapons.aspx?ID=71)_ draws forth the power of the earth itself to strike at enemies. **Activate** Interact; **Frequency** once per day; **Effect** You hurl the _stoneraiser javelin_ at an unoccupied space within 30 feet. The javelin’s power flows into the ground, which erupts in a 5-foot-radius burst of razor-sharp stalagmites. The stalagmites are 10 feet tall. Everything on each side of the stalagmites has cover from creatures on the opposite side, and the stalagmites’ spaces are difficult terrain. A creature takes 4d4 piercing damage for every move action it takes that enters at least one of the affected spaces. Each 5-foot-by-5-foot section has AC 10, Hardness 14, and 30 Hit Points. It’s immune to critical hits and precision damage. A destroyed section can be moved through freely. The stalagmites crumble to dust after 1 minute. Any creature in the area when the stalagmites appear is pushed to the nearest unoccupied space of its choice; this movement is [forced movement](/Rules.aspx?ID=451). Pushed creatures must attempt a DC 28 Reflex saving throw or take 4d4 piercing damage. **Critical Success** The creature is unaffected.
**Success** The creature takes half damage.
**Failure** The creature takes full damage.
**Critical Failure** The creature takes double damage and is knocked [prone](/Conditions.aspx?ID=31).
name: Stoneraiser Javelin

    level="1"
    right="Item 2"
    pfs=""
>
[Toad Tears](/Equipment.aspx?ID=650)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Alchemical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=6" />
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />
<trait label="Ingested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=95" />
<trait label="Poison" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=126" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Bestiary 2](/Sources.aspx?ID=32) pg. 261

**Price** 6 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="Single Action" /><row>Interact</row></row>

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

Toad tears can be mixed with any other foodstuff or drink, but the poison can also be ingested as is. The process of refining giant toad poison lessens its deadly qualities, and as a result, toad tears are rarely directly fatal. Yet those who are exposed to toad tears often lose control of their actions, making it a dangerous poison nevertheless.

 **Saving Throw** DC 19 Fortitude; **Onset** 5 minutes; **Maximum Duration** 30 minutes; **Stage 1** [stupefied 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=37) (10 minutes); **Stage 2** stupefied 1 and [confused](/Conditions.aspx?ID=5) (1 minute)

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

The delicate process of extracting violet venom from a violet fungus leaves it diluted at the best of times. Alchemists are still on the hunt for a truly pure, unadulterated version of this highly toxic poison.

[Blightburn Ward](/Equipment.aspx?ID=653)
</title>

<spoilers>May contain spoilers from Extinction Curse</spoilers>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Abjuration" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=2" />

<column gap="tiny">

**Price** 2,300 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This heavy iron necklace is decorated with tiny gems that resemble the stars in the ceiling high above the Vault of the Black Desert. While you have invested this item, you cannot contract blightburn sickness. It doesn't cure [blightburn sickness](/Diseases.aspx?ID=21) if you already have it, but the disease's stage can't progress while you wear it.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Blightburn Ward

May contain spoilers from Extinction Curse **Source** [Pathfinder #155: Lord of the Black Sands](/Sources.aspx?ID=33) pg. 75 **Price** 2,800 gp **Usage** held in 1 hand **Bulk** 1 **Base Weapon** [Spear](/Weapons.aspx?ID=10) --- The tip of this _+2 greater striking [spear](/Weapons.aspx?ID=10)_ is large and wickedly barbed, and the haft is inlaid with fine silver lines that run the length of the weapon. Wielded by specially trained [xulgath](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=102) cavalry, _anchor spears_ are used to prevent the escape of gogiteths or other monsters capable of climbing or burrowing. **Activate** command; **Effect** The _anchor spear_ extends into a _+2 greater striking [longspear](/Weapons.aspx?ID=6)_ or back to its original form as a _+2 greater striking spear_. **Activate** command; **Frequency** once per 10 minutes; **Trigger** You damage a creature using the _anchor spear_; **Effect** The spear embeds itself into the creature, and the spear's haft splits along the silver lines to become a series of hooks attached to a thin but strong cable. As long as the _anchor spear_ remains embedded in the creature, any time the creature burrows or climbs, it treats normal terrain as difficult terrain and difficult terrain as greater difficult terrain. The creature can spend an Interact action to attempt a DC 32 [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) check to remove the spearhead. **Critical Success** The _anchor spear_ is removed, becomes [broken](/Conditions.aspx?ID=2), and falls to the ground in the creature's space.
**Success** The _anchor spear_ is removed, the creature takes 1d8 piercing damage, and the spear falls to the ground in a random space adjacent to the creature.
**Failure** The _anchor spear_ remains in place, and the creature takes 1d8 piercing damage.
**Critical Failure** As failure, except the creature takes 2d8 piercing damage.
name: Anchor Spear

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 13+"
    pfs=""
>
[Cave Worm Repellent](/Equipment.aspx?ID=655)
</title>

<spoilers>May contain spoilers from Extinction Curse</spoilers>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Alchemical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=6" />
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Pathfinder #155: Lord of the Black Sands](/Sources.aspx?ID=33) pg. 75

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="" /><row>1 minute (Interact)</row></row>

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

Cave worm repellent is a highly noxious oil that can be applied to a creature or sprinkled in a circle around a 10-foot-radius area. In either case, after it is applied, it lasts for 24 hours or until it is scrubbed clean with 1 minute of work.

 When applied to a creature, the repellent causes [cave worms](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=21) of the indicated type to avoid attacking that creature in melee as long as they have something else nearby to attack. The repellent does not deter a cave worm from making ranged attacks against a coated target. If no creatures are within a cave worm's reach and it wants to approach and make a melee attack, it approaches and attacks a non-coated creature if it can without spending more actions to do so. 

If the coated creature is swallowed by the designated type of cave worm, the cave worm must succeed at a Fortitude save or involuntarily regurgitate the swallowed creature on the next round (a single action).

 When sprinkled around an area, cave worm repellent negates any chance of a random encounter with the particular type of cave worm in that area, as the creatures purposefully avoid the affected area.

 Other types of repellent that function similarly to this one might exist at the GM's discretion. Uncommon or rare creatures are typically much harder to repel, and repellent against them might necessitate harvesting ingredients from the creatures themselves (such as the creature's liver or a certain gland).

</column>

markdown:
<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 13+"
    pfs=""
>
[Cave Worm Repellent](/Equipment.aspx?ID=655)
</title>

<spoilers>May contain spoilers from Extinction Curse</spoilers>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Alchemical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=6" />
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Pathfinder #155: Lord of the Black Sands](/Sources.aspx?ID=33) pg. 75

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="" /><row>1 minute (Interact)</row></row>

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

Cave worm repellent is a highly noxious oil that can be applied to a creature or sprinkled in a circle around a 10-foot-radius area. In either case, after it is applied, it lasts for 24 hours or until it is scrubbed clean with 1 minute of work.

 When applied to a creature, the repellent causes [cave worms](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=21) of the indicated type to avoid attacking that creature in melee as long as they have something else nearby to attack. The repellent does not deter a cave worm from making ranged attacks against a coated target. If no creatures are within a cave worm's reach and it wants to approach and make a melee attack, it approaches and attacks a non-coated creature if it can without spending more actions to do so. 

If the coated creature is swallowed by the designated type of cave worm, the cave worm must succeed at a Fortitude save or involuntarily regurgitate the swallowed creature on the next round (a single action).

 When sprinkled around an area, cave worm repellent negates any chance of a random encounter with the particular type of cave worm in that area, as the creatures purposefully avoid the affected area.

 Other types of repellent that function similarly to this one might exist at the GM's discretion. Uncommon or rare creatures are typically much harder to repel, and repellent against them might necessitate harvesting ingredients from the creatures themselves (such as the creature's liver or a certain gland).

May contain spoilers from Extinction Curse **Source** [Pathfinder #155: Lord of the Black Sands](/Sources.aspx?ID=33) pg. 75 **Price** 2,500 gp **Usage** held in 1 hand **Bulk** L **Activate**Interact --- This watery, mint-green emetic of [xulgath](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=102) design is often carried in a delicate obsidian vial or other fragile container. In the unfortunate circumstance that a creature has been [Swallowed Whole](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=35) by another creature, the swallowed creature can break open a vial of greengut to immediately induce vomiting. The swallower must succeed at a DC 40 Fortitude save or take 18d6 poison damage and possibly vomit the contents of its stomach. **Critical Success** The creature is unaffected.
**Success** The creature takes half damage.
**Failure** The creature takes full damage, is [sickened 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=34), and vomits up any and all creatures Swallowed Whole on its next turn (a single action, which also allows it a Fortitude save against the sickened condition), as well as any stones or other objects it swallowed.
**Critical Failure** As failure, but the creature takes double damage and is sickened 2.
name: Green Gut

    level="1"
    right="Item 13"
    pfs="Limited"
>
[Bomber's Saddle](/Equipment.aspx?ID=657)
</title>

<spoilers>May contain spoilers from Extinction Curse</spoilers>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Companion" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=29" />
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=98" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Pathfinder #155: Lord of the Black Sands](/Sources.aspx?ID=33) pg. 76

**Price** 2,800 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** worn saddle

**Bulk** 2
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

Developed by [urdefhans](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=158) for their [skaveling](/Monsters.aspx?ID=803) mounts but quickly copied by other races' cavalry, this leather saddle is made from [cave worm](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=21) hide and is designed to fit a variety of flying steeds. In addition to a seat for the rider, the _bomber's saddle_ has a bomb compartment situated underneath the mount. The compartment can hold up to six [alchemical bombs](/Equipment.aspx?Category=6&Subcategory=7) of light or negligible Bulk.

**Activate** <actions string="Single Action" /> Interact;     **Effect** You drop a single item from the saddle's compartment, which falls to the ground below. If the item is a [bomb](/WeaponGroups.aspx?ID=2), make a ranged Strike with it.

**Activate** <actions string="Two Actions" /> Interact;    **Requirements** The saddle's compartment is loaded with two to six bombs; **Effect** You drop the saddle's entire payload, scattering the bombs below. Make a single ranged Strike against an AC of 10. The AC increases by 1 for every 5 feet above the target area you are. On a failure, the bombs fly away wildly and deal no damage. On a successful attack roll, the bombs fall and shatter, starting in the target area and moving in a line of 5-foot spaces up to 30 feet long for six bombs, one bomb per space. The bombs don't directly hit any creatures; instead, creatures take [splash](/Traits.aspx?ID=150) damage from the bombs as usual, but combine the splash damage from multiple overlapping bombs together before applying weaknesses or resistances. Apply any effects normally applied to splash damage when throwing a bomb (such as the effects of the [Calculated Splash](/Feats.aspx?ID=99) feat).

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Bomber's Saddle

[Heedless Spurs](/Equipment.aspx?ID=658)
</title>

<spoilers>May contain spoilers from Extinction Curse</spoilers>

<traits>
<trait label="Rare" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=137" />
<trait label="Abjuration" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=2" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Pathfinder #155: Lord of the Black Sands](/Sources.aspx?ID=33) pg. 76

**Price** 5,200 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** worn anklets

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

These wicked-looking spiked metal wheels fit around the ankles and jingle when the wearer walks, imposing a –1 item penalty on [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=15) checks. If used as a weapon, they are treated as a [spiked gauntlet](/Weapons.aspx?ID=11).

**Activate** <actions string="Single Action" /> Interact;  **Frequency** once per 10 minutes;  **Requirements** You are riding a mount; **Effect** You kick both spurs into your mount's flank. Your mount takes 2d6 [persistent bleed damage](/Conditions.aspx?ID=29) and gains the [quickened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=32) condition for 1 minute or until the persistent bleed damage ends, whichever comes first. It can use the extra action only to Stride.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Heedless Spurs

    level="1"
    right="Item 18"
    pfs=""
>
[Life's Last Breath](/Equipment.aspx?ID=659)
</title>

<spoilers>May contain spoilers from Extinction Curse</spoilers>

<traits>
<trait label="Unique" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=161" />
<trait label="Evil" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=64" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
<trait label="Necromancy" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=117" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Pathfinder #155: Lord of the Black Sands](/Sources.aspx?ID=33) pg. 76

**Price** 23,500 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

**Bulk** 2
</row>

<row gap="medium">
**Base Weapon** [Rhoka Sword](/Weapons.aspx?ID=120)
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

The twin serrated blades of this legendary [rhoka sword](/Weapons.aspx?ID=120) are dull black and constantly drip with blood. _Life's Last Breath_ is said to have been forged centuries ago for an unknown [urdefhan](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=158) general and has since fallen into the hands of countless other powerful urdefhan warlords.

 _Life's Last Breath_ is a _+3 greater striking [unholy](/Equipment.aspx?ID=307) [wounding](/Equipment.aspx?ID=309) rhoka sword_ with the following special abilities.

**Activate** <actions string="Reaction" /> command;  **Frequency** once per day;  **Requirements** You successfully Strike a creature with _Life's Last Breath_; **Effect** You cast a 9th-level [_paralyze_](/Spells.aspx?ID=213) spell on the target.

**Activate** <actions string="Reaction" /> envision;  **Frequency** once per 10 minutes; **Trigger** You successfully Strike a creature with _Life's Last Breath_;  **Effect** The target must succeed at a DC 38 Fortitude save or take 4d6 negative damage; you gain an equal amount of temporary Hit Points that last 1 minute.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Life's Last Breath

</title>

<spoilers>May contain spoilers from Extinction Curse</spoilers>

<traits>
<trait label="Rare" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=137" />
<trait label="Alchemical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=6" />
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />
<trait label="Elixir" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=59" />
<trait label="Necromancy" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=117" />

---

This elixir is usually stored in a small metal flask or miniature stein. The dark liquid sparkles as if it were full of stars and is considered sacred to [Cayden Cailean](/Deities.aspx?ID=4) and to the cults of the Failed in Absalom. The next time you would die from damage within 8 hours after you drink the ambrosia, it prevents you from dying, you regain 20 Hit Points, the elixir's benefits end, and you become temporarily immune to the _ambrosia of undying hope_ for 24 hours. The ambrosia can't protect you from [death](/Traits.aspx?ID=40) effects or disintegration, but it can protect against almost any other form of death via damage. You can only be under the effect of a single _ambrosia of undying hope_ at a time.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Ambrosia of Undying Hope

This strand of hair shimmers like the strands of fate. When you activate the string, it resonates with a single, perfect note. This _+2 [greater resilient](/Equipment.aspx?ID=279) [studded leather](/Armor.aspx?ID=5)_ is traditionally inscribed with a symbol of either [Norgorber](/Deities.aspx?ID=13) or [Cayden Cailean](/Deities.aspx?ID=4). Its leather is light as silk, and its buttons and studs are gleaming crystal. While you wear it, you get a +15-foot item bonus to your Speed, and effects that attempt to impose penalties on your Speed must first [counteract](/Rules.aspx?ID=371) the _jerkin of liberation_ or they have no effect on you.

In addition, you are hard to incapacitate. You use the jerkin's item level or your own level, whichever is higher, to determine effects of the [incapacitation](/Traits.aspx?ID=93) trait.

[Locket of Sealed Nightmares](/Equipment.aspx?ID=664)
</title>

<spoilers>May contain spoilers from Extinction Curse</spoilers>

<traits>
<trait label="Rare" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=137" />
<trait label="Illusion" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=92" />

---

As long as this locket of horn and silver is closed, you don't need to sleep. However, you can still overexert your body with too much activity, so you still need to rest to avoid becoming [fatigued](/Conditions.aspx?ID=15) or to remove the fatigued condition. You also gain a +4 item bonus on saving throws against mental illusions, magical effects that would make you [unconscious](/Conditions.aspx?ID=38), and effects that would make you fatigued. Each time dawn occurs, you regain the ability to make your daily preparations as if you had rested for 8 hours. Daily preparations still take about an hour, as normal.

**Activate** <actions string="Two Actions" /> envision, Interact;    **Requirements** You aren't fatigued; **Effect** You open the locket to unleash the dreams it has kept at bay. You become fatigued and cast one of the following spells at 9th level (DC 41): [_hallucination_](/Spells.aspx?ID=144), [_sleep_](/Spells.aspx?ID=288), or [_weird_](/Spells.aspx?ID=375).

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Locket of Sealed Nightmares

This fruit is slowly, constantly changing shape, transforming from one fruit into another, but always appearing at the peak of ripeness. When you consume the fruit, it attempts to [counteract](/Rules.aspx?ID=371) all curses, diseases, and poisons affecting you, and you immediately regain 8d8+30 Hit Points. The fruit has a counteract level of 9 and a +30 modifier for the rolls against each condition. The fruit's seed remains after consumption and can be planted later, becoming a seed-shaped [_tree feather token_](/Equipment.aspx?ID=244) that sprouts a tree bearing many kinds of mundane fruit.

>
[Piereta](/Equipment.aspx?ID=666)
</title>

<spoilers>May contain spoilers from Extinction Curse</spoilers>

<traits>
<trait label="Unique" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=161" />
<trait label="LG" url="/Rules.aspx?ID=95" />
<trait label="Divine" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=48" />
<trait label="Evocation" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=65" />
<trait label="Intelligent" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=280" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Pathfinder #156: The Apocalypse Prophet](/Sources.aspx?ID=36) pg. 74

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

**Bulk** 1
</row>

<row gap="medium">
**Base Weapon** [Rapier](/Weapons.aspx?ID=36)
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

**Perception** +27; precise vision 30 feet, imprecise hearing 30 feet

**Communication** telepathy ([Azlanti](/Languages.aspx?ID=49), [Celestial](/Traits.aspx?ID=23), [Common](/Traits.aspx?ID=28), [Utopian](/Languages.aspx?ID=36)) 

**Skills** [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=6) +33, [Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=13) +29, [Warfare Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=8) +27

**Int** +0, **Wis** +4, **Cha** +6

**Will** +31 

</column>

---        

Piereta was a Knight of Ozem during the Shining Crusade, sworn to the service of Aroden's herald, [Arazni](/Deities.aspx?ID=31). After Arazni perished at the hands of the Whispering Tyrant, Piereta became the first paladin to swear her service to the newly ascended goddess, [Iomedae](/Deities.aspx?ID=9). When she fell in battle, a fragment of her spirit clung to the sword through which she had wielded her faith in countless battles, granting the weapon a sentience of its own. The sword named herself after her fallen bearer and has been a faithful companion to many Iomedaean warriors since.

 _Piereta_ is a _+3 [major striking](/Equipment.aspx?ID=280) [holy](/Equipment.aspx?ID=299) [rapier](/Weapons.aspx?ID=36)_ devoted to the principles of Iomedae as an exemplar of valorous action and as a protector of the innocent.

 _Piereta_ is very particular about who she permits to wield her. She agrees to work with you if you are a follower of Iomedae, or if you are lawful good or neutral good and worship a different good deity. Otherwise, you must convince _Piereta_ of the rightness of your cause. If _Piereta_ doesn't want you to wield her, once per minute she can attempt to force you to put her down (DC 42 Will save to resist this command). Even if you succeed at this saving throw, Piereta refuses to apply any of her fundamental or property runes and gives you a –2 penalty on attack rolls with _Piereta_ or any other weapon until you drop her.

 _Piereta_ can use the following activations.

**Activate** <actions string="Reaction" /> command, Interact;  **Frequency** once per minute;   **Effect** _Piereta_ makes a [Retributive Strike](/Actions.aspx?ID=5), resolved as though you had used the ability, including any benefits from champion feats you have.

**Activate** <actions string="Two Actions" /> command, Interact;  **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** _Piereta_ casts a 9th-level [_field of life_](/Spells.aspx?ID=114).

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Piereta

>
[Planar Ribbon](/Equipment.aspx?ID=670)
</title>

<spoilers>May contain spoilers from Extinction Curse</spoilers>

<traits>
<trait label="Unique" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=161" />
<trait label="Conjuration" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=33" />
<trait label="Occult" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=120" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Pathfinder #156: The Apocalypse Prophet](/Sources.aspx?ID=36) pg. 75

**Price** 60,000 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

By twisting and twirling this ordinary-looking leather ribbon, you can temporarily bend a rift in space.

**Activate** <actions string="" /> 1 minute (command, envision, Interact);     **Effect** The ribbon spins and swirls, creating a circular loop in the air through which you can spy on a creature on another plane. This has the effects of a [_scrying_](/Spells.aspx?ID=268) spell (DC 40) against the creature but functions more as a mundane —albeit interplanar—window; a similar loop appears before the target, and for the duration, both sides can clearly see and hear each other through the created portal, but nothing can pass through. You can Sustain the Activation to gain the effects of Sustaining the Spell. This activation has the [scrying](/Traits.aspx?ID=141) trait.

**Activate** <actions string="Single Action" /> Interact;  **Frequency** once per round;   **Effect** You extend the ribbon to brush against a foe up to 60 feet away. If that creature fails a DC 42 Reflex save, you exchange positions with the target and all of that creature's allies are [flat-footed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=16) to you until the end of your turn. This activation has the [teleportation](/Traits.aspx?ID=156) trait.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Planar Ribbon

</title>

<spoilers>May contain spoilers from Extinction Curse</spoilers>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Evocation" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=65" />

This [high-grade orichalcum shield](/Equipment.aspx?ID=315) (Hardness 16, HP 64, and BT 32) bears a _+3 [major striking](/Equipment.aspx?ID=280) [shield boss](/Weapons.aspx?ID=41)_ that cannot be removed. It gleams brightly in any light, drawing attention at all times. When you Raise this Shield, ephemeral reflections of it appear in front of each of your allies within 30 feet, granting them its +2 circumstance bonus to AC for as long as they are within 30 feet of you and your shield is raised.

**Activate** <actions string="Two Actions" /> Interact ([force](/Traits.aspx?ID=75)) **Frequency** once per minute;   **Effect** You heft the shield and thrust it forward, causing ephemeral reflections of the shield to surround you and then rush outward in a 90-foot line or 60-foot cone (your choice). Make a [shield bash](/Weapons.aspx?ID=40) Strike against each enemy within the area, except that the damage dealt is force damage. Each target you hit is moved 10 feet away from you (each target you critically hit is also knocked [prone](/Conditions.aspx?ID=31)). This is [forced movement](/Rules.aspx?ID=451). These shield bashes count against your multiple attack penalty, but the penalty doesn't increase until after you have made all these attacks.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Shield of the Unified Legion

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 25"
    pfs=""
>
[Aroden's Hearthstone](/Equipment.aspx?ID=673)
</title>

<spoilers>May contain spoilers from Extinction Curse</spoilers>

<traits>
<trait label="Unique" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=161" />
<trait label="Arcane" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=11" />
<trait label="Artifact" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=253" />
<trait label="Enchantment" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=61" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Pathfinder #156: The Apocalypse Prophet](/Sources.aspx?ID=36) pg. 66

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This coin-sized gemstone can be used on its own but must be included in the construction of a hearth in order to fully activate its magic.

**Activate** <actions string="" /> 3 days (Interact);  **Frequency** 4 times per year;  **Requirements** You have Legendary proficiency in [Crafting](/Skills.aspx?ID=4); **Effect** You craft a magical weapon or armor of up to 20th level. You must attempt a Crafting check as usual to create the item, but you need supply only raw materials worth a quarter of the item's Price, and you need not pay the remaining portion of the item's Price or spend additional downtime days working on it. While _Aroden's Hearthstone_ is active, the stars in the sky, including Golarion's sun, appear to glow brighter within a 100-mile radius of the hearthstone.

**Activate** <actions string="Three Actions" /> command, envision;  **Frequency** once per month;   **Effect** _Aroden's Hearthstone_ draws in heat from the world itself, causing the ambient temperature within 100 miles to drop below 0°F. Within 1 mile of the _Hearthstone_, the temperature drops to –200°F, rivaling the frigid cold of outer space and dealing 4d6 cold damage each round to every creature in the area. This effect lasts 1 minute, during which time the nearest moon (probably Golarion's) appears an eerie blue color to any observers within 100 miles of the hearthstone. At the end of 1 minute, the hearthstone emits a blast of concentrated heat that deals 25d6 fire damage to creatures and objects within 150 feet. 

**Destruction** Using Aroden_'s Hearthstone_ to craft a cursed item results in a failed creation and causes the gemstone to split into five equal-sized but magically impotent pieces. Over the course of a year, four of theses pieces crumble to dust, while the fifth expands to the size of the original artifact and bears its powers anew. To destroy _Aroden's Hearthstone_, each of the separated pieces must be plunged into one of the five stars that compose the cosmic constellation known as the Follower.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Aroden's Hearthstone

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 23"
    pfs=""
>
[Hunter's Dawn](/Equipment.aspx?ID=674)
</title>

<spoilers>May contain spoilers from Extinction Curse</spoilers>

<traits>
<trait label="Unique" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=161" />
<trait label="Arcane" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=11" />
<trait label="Artifact" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=253" />
<trait label="Enchantment" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=61" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Pathfinder #156: The Apocalypse Prophet](/Sources.aspx?ID=36) pg. 67

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

**Bulk** 1
</row>

<row gap="medium">
**Base Weapon** [Club](/Weapons.aspx?ID=2)
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

_Hunter's Dawn_ is a _+3 [greater striking](/Equipment.aspx?ID=280) [speed](/Equipment.aspx?ID=304) [ghost touch](/Equipment.aspx?ID=297) [club](/Weapons.aspx?ID=2)_. However, the notched head of the weapon marks it as an atlatl, an ancient tool used for throwing [darts](/Weapons.aspx?ID=68) and [javelins](/Weapons.aspx?ID=71). You can use _Hunter's Dawn_ with a mundane dart or javelin like you would use a bow with an arrow; when you do so, the range of the dart or javelin is doubled, and the dart or javelin uses _Hunter's Dawn_ to determine its attack modifier and damage dice.

**Activate** <actions string="Single Action to Three Actions" /> Interact;     **Effect** You swing _Hunter's Dawn_ and launch a magically conjured javelin at a target within 60 feet, making a ranged Strike. The javelin has the statistics of a javelin but gains the magical properties of _Hunter's Dawn_ and disappears immediately after hitting or missing its target. On a successful hit, you deal damage (3d6 piercing, due to the atlatl's _greater striking_ rune, plus your Strength modifier); in addition, a gazelle leaps from the hit target to one or more targets within 20 feet of the first, dealing 2d6 piercing damage plus your Strength modifier. The number of additional targets hit in this way is equal to the number of actions you spend to activate this ability (one additional target if you used one action, two additional targets for two actions, and so forth).

**Activate** <actions string="Reaction" /> ;   **Trigger** You hit a creature with a ranged Strike with _Hunter's Dawn_;  **Effect** The target is marked. Once per day, you can cast a 5th-level [_locate_](/Spells.aspx?ID=173) spell on the marked target, regardless of its distance from you or your ability to cast spells. This effect persists until it is dispelled (counterspell DC 46), the creature dies, or a new target is marked.

**Activate** <actions string="Single Action" /> Interact;  **Frequency** three times per day;   **Effect** You shave a curl of wood from _Hunter's Dawn_ and create a roaring fire within 15 feet. You can choose one of the following shapes for the fire to take: a 10-foot square, a 20-foot line, or a 15-foot cone. Each creature in the affected squares take 6d6 fire damage (DC 46 basic Reflex save).

**Destruction** _Hunter's Dawn_ can be destroyed only by placing it on the funeral pyre of a dead god.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Hunter's Dawn

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 22"
    pfs=""
>
[Kortos Diamond](/Equipment.aspx?ID=675)
</title>

<spoilers>May contain spoilers from Extinction Curse</spoilers>

<traits>
<trait label="Unique" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=161" />
<trait label="Artifact" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=253" />
<trait label="Divine" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=48" />
<trait label="Evocation" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=65" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Pathfinder #156: The Apocalypse Prophet](/Sources.aspx?ID=36) pg. 68

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

**Bulk** 1
</row>

<row gap="medium">
**Base Weapon** [Longsword](/Weapons.aspx?ID=31)
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

The hilt of this _+3 [major striking](/Equipment.aspx?ID=280) [axiomatic](/Equipment.aspx?ID=291) [spell-storing](/Equipment.aspx?ID=305) [longsword](/Weapons.aspx?ID=31)_ is simple and elegant. The blade itself is made of throneglass—a clear, jewellike crafting material with a razor-sharp edge, capable of absorbing psychic magic and redirecting it at the wielder's enemies. Whenever the _Kortos Diamond_ is in an area of dim or brighter light, it glows faintly, as if reflecting the light of a setting sun.

 While wielding the Kortos Diamond, you gain a +2 item bonus on saving throws against [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=106) effects. You can never harm yourself with the Kortos Diamond, even if [confused](/Conditions.aspx?ID=5).

 If you ever travel farther than 100 miles from the Isle of Kortos while in possession of the _Kortos Diamond_, the sword becomes impossibly heavy; you are [encumbered](/Conditions.aspx?ID=12) as long as you carry the sword and remain outside of this area. If you relinquish the _Kortos Diamond_ while away from Kortos, the sword gradually makes its way back to the isle over the course of a year.

**Activate** <actions string="Single Action" /> Interact;     **Effect** You attempt a melee Strike with the _Kortos Diamond_ against a creature within reach. On a hit, you deal damage as normal and the target's mind is inflicted with a powerful disruptive energy. When the creature attempts to Cast a Spell, it must attempt a DC 44 Will save; on a failure, the spell fails and both the spell and the actions spent to cast it are lost. This effect lasts for up to 1 minute.

**Activate** <actions string="Free Action" /> envision;  **Frequency** once per round; **Trigger** You damage an [aberration](/Traits.aspx?ID=1) with the _Kortos Diamond_;  **Effect** You deal an additional 2d8 force damage to the struck creature.

**Activate** <actions string="Two Actions" /> command, Interact;  **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** You hold the _Kortos Diamond_ aloft and proclaim your might in a booming voice. You cast [_overwhelming presence_](/Spells.aspx?ID=212) (DC 42).

**Destruction** A deity who ascended to divinity via the Test of the Starstone can damage and destroy the _Kortos Diamond_ as if it were not an artifact.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Kortos Diamond

markdown:
May contain spoilers from Extinction Curse **Source** [Pathfinder #156: The Apocalypse Prophet](/Sources.aspx?ID=36) pg. 69 **Usage** worn necklace **Bulk** L --- This gold necklace sports six bails, each with a different _[aeon stone](/Equipment.aspx?ID=407)_. The backside of each bail is engraved with a single ancient [Azlanti](/Languages.aspx?ID=49) rune: Patience, Remembrance, Resilience, Tenacity, Wisdom, Invention. While a bail is active, you recall pieces of childhood memories, brief stories and lessons spoken to you by Aroden. The knowledge imparted by each bail carries a specific power but also allows you to access the invested and resonant powers of the _aeon stone_ in the active bail's slot as though the stone orbited your head. Each day at dawn, a new bail becomes active; roll 1d6 to determine which bail is active, determined by the table below. Only one bail can be active at any time. Once per day, you can attempt to exchange the _aeon stone_ in a given bail for another _aeon stone_ with a successful DC 35 [Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=2) check. ##
**D6****Bail****Default _Aeon Stone_**
1PatienceGold nodule
**Active Power**: Telepathy with a range of 100 feet
2RemembranceTourmaline sphere
**Active Power**: Cast [_hypercognition_](/Spells.aspx?ID=156) at will
3ResilienceClear spindle
**Active Power**: Worn armor gains the [_antimagic_](/Equipment.aspx?ID=282) property rune
4TenacityPink rhomboid
**Active Power**: Wielded weapons gain the [_vorpal_](/Equipment.aspx?ID=308) property rune
5WisdomOrange prism
**Active Power**: Skill checks for [downtime](/Traits.aspx?ID=49) activities or to [Recall Knowledge](/Actions.aspx?ID=26) are one degree of success higher
6InventionLavender and green ellipsoid
**Active Power**: You can craft items up to 5 levels higher than your actual level
 **Destruction** The memories and lessons that echo through the _Tears of the Last Azlanti_ give clues about steps needed to destroy the artifact. After you have gained the power of each of the six aeon stones at least once, you are granted visions of six locations across Golarion. At each of these locations, you must replace one of the necklace's aeon stones with a _dull gray aeon stone_. Once the final stone is replaced, the powers of the necklace are drawn into its new stones. These stones become pieces of throneglass, and the chain and bails disintegrate.
name: Tears of the Last Azlanti

</title>

<traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Advanced Player's Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=39) pg. 249

**Price** 1 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Hands** 2

**Bulk** 1
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

---

You can use a net either on its own or attached to a rope. When attached to a rope, you can use the net to [Grapple](/Actions.aspx?ID=35) a Medium or smaller creature up to 10 feet away (instead of only adjacent creatures). You can continue to Grapple to keep your hold on the target so long as the target remains within 10 feet and you continue to hold the net. The [grabbed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=20) creature gains a +2 circumstance bonus to [Escape](/Actions.aspx?ID=79) unless you are adjacent to them, and it can attempt a DC 16 [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) check to [Force Open](/Actions.aspx?ID=34) the net entirely. Once the target is no longer grabbed, the net is unwieldy until refolded as a [Concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=32) action that requires two hands; if used without being refolded, Grapple checks made with the net take a –2 penalty. 

When the net is unattached, you can attempt a ranged attack roll against a Medium or smaller creature within 20 feet. On a hit, the target is [flat-footed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=16) and takes a –10- foot circumstance penalty to its Speeds until it Escapes, and on a critical hit, it's also [immobilized](/Conditions.aspx?ID=24) until it Escapes. The Escape DC is 16. A creature adjacent to the target can Interact to remove the net.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Net

>
[Crystal Shards](/Equipment.aspx?ID=685)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Alchemical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=6" />
<trait label="Bomb" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=21" />
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />
<trait label="Earth" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=55" />
<trait label="Splash" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=150" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Advanced Player's Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=39) pg. 252

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="Single Action" /><row>Strike</row></row>

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This flask holds a pressurized red-brown gas flecked with bits of sublimated crystal. You gain the listed item bonus to attack rolls. When the bomb explodes, it deals the listed piercing damage and piercing splash damage as the mixture suddenly turns into solid crystals flying at high speeds.

 Surfaces in the splash area grow brittle, gleaming red crystals that crumble to dust after 1 minute. The crystals on level surfaces are [caltrops](/Equipment.aspx?ID=9). The crystals on vertical surfaces can be used briefly as handholds, granting the listed item bonus to the next [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) check to [Climb](/Actions.aspx?ID=33) each space. The crystals in each space crumble after the creature leaves, whether Climbing or moving through the caltrops.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Crystal Shards

---

These spring-loaded metal canisters contain a mixture of chemicals and salts that drain and disintegrate nearby [undead](/Traits.aspx?ID=160) creatures. A ghost charge deals the listed positive damage and splash damage, though as usual for positive damage, this damage harms only undead and creatures with [negative healing](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=42). Ghost charges are designed to explode even on contact with a spiritual substance, making them ideal for damaging [incorporeal](/Traits.aspx?ID=222) undead. A primary target that takes damage from a ghost charge becomes [enfeebled](/Conditions.aspx?ID=13) until the start of your next turn. Many types grant an item bonus to attack rolls.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Ghost Charge

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 0"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Drakeheart Mutagen](/Equipment.aspx?ID=688)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Alchemical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=6" />
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />
<trait label="Elixir" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=59" />
<trait label="Mutagen" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=116" />
<trait label="Polymorph" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=127" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Advanced Player's Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=39) pg. 253

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="Single Action" /><row>Interact</row></row>

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

Your skin grows tough scales like a [drake](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=36), your eyesight become sharp and your pupils slitted, and your limbs grow wiry and quick, but your mind and reflexes become slow. 

**Benefit** You gain the listed bonus to AC, a Dexterity cap of +2 (as usual, use your lowest Dexterity cap if you have more than one), and an item bonus to Perception checks. If you're wearing armor, you still calculate your proficiency bonus to AC based on your proficiency in the armor you're wearing, even if the drakeheart mutagen has a higher item bonus. You also gain the Final Surge action.

**Activate** <actions string="Single Action" /> Final Surge;     **Effect** You Stride twice. The drakeheart mutagen's duration ends.

 **Drawback** You take a –1 penalty to Will saves, Reflex saves, and all skill checks to [Recall Knowledge](/Actions.aspx?ID=26).

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Drakeheart Mutagen

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="Single Action" /><row>Interact</row></row>

 

 

 
</row>

---

Focus briefly calms your neurons, helping ease mental effects. When you drink this elixir, the elixir attempts a [counteract](/Rules.aspx?ID=371) check with the listed counteract modifier to remove the [confused](/Conditions.aspx?ID=5) or [stupefied](/Conditions.aspx?ID=37) condition from a single source, using the source of that condition to determine the counteract level and DC.

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 3+"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Olfactory Obfuscator](/Equipment.aspx?ID=690)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Alchemical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=6" />
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />
<trait label="Elixir" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=59" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Advanced Player's Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=39) pg. 254

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="Single Action" /><row>Interact</row></row>

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This frothing fluid causes the drinker's body to exude trace odor-absorbing oils. For the listed duration after drinking this elixir, your scent is nearly unnoticeable. Creatures with imprecise scent don't detect you with that sense unless they [Seek](/Actions.aspx?ID=84) for you, and you are [concealed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=4) from creatures that perceive you with precise scent. You gain a +4 item bonus against attempts to Seek you by creatures using only scent-based senses; if they use any other senses as well, the bonus doesn't apply.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

<title
level="2"
right="Item 3"
pfs="Standard"
>
Olfactory Obfuscator
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Advanced Player's Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=39) pg. 254

**Price** 9 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Bulk** L

</row>

</column>

---

The duration is 10 minutes.

<title
level="2"
right="Item 10"
pfs="Standard"
>
Olfactory Obfuscator (Greater)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Advanced Player's Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=39) pg. 254

**Price** 225 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Bulk** L

</row>

</column>

---

The duration is 8 hours.
name: Olfactory Obfuscator

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 16"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Cerulean Scourge](/Equipment.aspx?ID=692)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Alchemical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=6" />
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />
<trait label="Injury" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=97" />
<trait label="Poison" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=126" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Advanced Player's Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=39) pg. 254

**Price** 1,450 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 2 hands

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="Two Actions" /><row>Interact</row></row>

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

Made from a carefully distilled blend of serpentine hemotoxins, the fast-acting cerulean scourge is infamous for transforming the blood vessels near the original wound, which glow with a bright blue light before painfully bursting.

**Saving Throw** DC 36 Fortitude; **Maximum Duration** 6 rounds; **Stage 1** 9d6 poison damage (1 round); **Stage 2** 12d6 poison damage (1 round); **Stage 3** 15d6 poison damage (1 round)

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Cerulean Scourge

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 2+"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Bloodhound Mask](/Equipment.aspx?ID=694)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Alchemical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=6" />
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Advanced Player's Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=39) pg. 254

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="Single Action" /><row>Interact</row></row>

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This wide, single-use mask is designed to be slipped over your mouth and nose and its alchemical filter activated all with one hand. Once activated, the mask sharpens odors, giving you [imprecise scent](/Rules.aspx?ID=417) with the listed range. You can't wear other masks while you're wearing a bloodhound mask. When you use [Survival](/Skills.aspx?ID=16) to [Track](/Actions.aspx?ID=66) a creature by its scent, your proficiency bonus is equal to your level even if you're untrained, and the mask grants you the listed item bonus to your Survival check. The GM sets the Survival DC based on the area's ability to hold scent rather than on visual clues, as normal for using scent.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

level="2"
right="Item 2"
pfs="Standard"
>
Bloodhound Mask (Lesser)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Advanced Player's Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=39) pg. 254

**Price** 6 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Bulk** L

</row>

</column>

---

The range is 15 feet, the item bonus is +1, and the duration is 10 minutes.

<title
level="2"
right="Item 6"
pfs="Standard"
>
Bloodhound Mask (Moderate)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Advanced Player's Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=39) pg. 254

**Price** 40 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Bulk** L

</row>

</column>

---

The range is 30 feet, the item bonus is +2, and the duration is 1 hour.

<title
level="2"
right="Item 11"
pfs="Standard"
>
Bloodhound Mask (Greater)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Advanced Player's Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=39) pg. 254

**Price** 250 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Bulk** L

</row>

</column>

---

The range is 60 feet, the item bonus is +3, and the duration is 8 hours.
name: Bloodhound Mask

<trait label="Alchemical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=6" />
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Advanced Player's Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=39) pg. 254

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="Single Action" /><row>Interact</row></row>

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This wide, single-use mask is designed to be slipped over your mouth and nose and its alchemical filter activated all with one hand. Once activated, the mask sharpens odors, giving you [imprecise scent](/Rules.aspx?ID=417) with the listed range. You can't wear other masks while you're wearing a bloodhound mask. When you use [Survival](/Skills.aspx?ID=16) to [Track](/Actions.aspx?ID=66) a creature by its scent, your proficiency bonus is equal to your level even if you're untrained, and the mask grants you the listed item bonus to your Survival check. The GM sets the Survival DC based on the area's ability to hold scent rather than on visual clues, as normal for using scent.

    right="Item 1"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Forensic Dye](/Equipment.aspx?ID=695)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Alchemical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=6" />
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Advanced Player's Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=39) pg. 254

**Price** 3 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="Single Action" /><row>Interact</row></row>

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

Activating this vial of colorless liquid requires inserting a small amount of another chemical or material, such as blood or mud. The vial reacts rapidly, transforming into a murky, reddish-brown substance.

 Once activated, the dye remains potent for up to 10 minutes, during which time you can spend 1 minute to brush it onto a single object of up to 1 Bulk or across the ground in a single 5-foot square. Where the dye comes in contact with an exact match for the activating chemical, it takes on a bright blue hue, while staying transparent in areas where there is no activating component present.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Forensic Dye

    level="1"
    right="Item 1"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Ghost Ink](/Equipment.aspx?ID=696)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Alchemical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=6" />
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Advanced Player's Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=39) pg. 255

**Price** 3 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><row>10 minutes (Interact)</row></row>

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This pale-blue ink dries rapidly, becoming fully transparent 1 minute after application. The ink glows red when exposed to heat, such as that from a [torch](/Equipment.aspx?ID=60) or other open flame. This glow lasts only as long as the ink is exposed to heat, after which the ink becomes invisible again. The crafter of the ghost ink can alter the formula slightly to instead make the ink sensitive to sunlight, starlight, magical light, or heatless light created by an alchemical effect such as a [sunrod](/Equipment.aspx?ID=137).

 While the text isn't glowing, a creature closely examining a surface marked with ghost ink can detect the presence of the ink with a successful DC 25 Perception check. On a critical success, they can make out the ink well enough to use [Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=14) to [Decipher Writing](/Actions.aspx?ID=22). One vial of ghost ink is sufficient to write a page worth of text.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Ghost Ink

</title>

<traits>

</row>

</column>

---

This shimmering, violet unguent forms mild chemical bonds between objects with a matching composition. You apply the adhesive to two objects, or to an object and a creature. You can check if the two share an origin (such as if they were broken from the same whole, or if a poison sample or body part came from the same creature) by holding them together with the unguent between; if they match, the unguent becomes sticky.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Origin Unguent

    pfs="Standard"
>
[Skinstitch Salve](/Equipment.aspx?ID=698)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Alchemical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=6" />
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />
<trait label="Healing" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=89" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Advanced Player's Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=39) pg. 255

**Price** 55 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This sticky salve stubbornly holds wounds closed and encourages swift natural healing. You can activate the salve in either of the following ways.

**Activate** <actions string="Single Action" /> Interact;     **Effect** You [Administer First Aid](/Actions.aspx?ID=54) without requiring [healer's tools](/Equipment.aspx?ID=25). You either gain a +2 item bonus to the [Medicine](/Skills.aspx?ID=9) check, or you can use the skinstitch salve's Medicine modifier of +13 instead of your own.

**Activate** <actions string="Free Action" /> Interact;   **Trigger** You [Treat Wounds](/Actions.aspx?ID=57) or use [Battle Medicine](/Feats.aspx?ID=760);  **Effect** You gain a +2 item bonus to the triggering [Medicine](/Skills.aspx?ID=9) check. If you roll a success on the Medicine check, you get a critical success instead.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Skinstitch Salve

    level="1"
    right="Item 7"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Sovereign Glue](/Equipment.aspx?ID=699)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Alchemical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=6" />
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Advanced Player's Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=39) pg. 255

**Price** 55 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><row>1 minute (Interact)</row></row>

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This peculiar amber glue bonds two surfaces together almost inseparably. A single flask covers an area up to 1 square foot and must be used all at once to form a single bond between two surfaces. If the activation is interrupted, the bond fails and the glue is wasted.

 Once two surfaces are joined with sovereign glue, they can be separated only with a successful DC 50 [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) check. The adhered objects tend to break before the glue does unless they're particularly durable, though a creature determined to separate the objects can break off the parts connected by the sovereign glue and later [Repair](/Actions.aspx?ID=42) the objects.

 Sovereign glue can affect creatures only if they are willing, and its bond can be harmlessly broken by carefully exfoliating the outermost layer of skin or waiting a short while for the skin to shed.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Sovereign Glue

    level="1"
    right="Item 4"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Timeless Salts](/Equipment.aspx?ID=700)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Alchemical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=6" />
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Advanced Player's Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=39) pg. 255

**Price** 14 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="Single Action" /><row>Interact</row></row>

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

You sprinkle these salts onto a single object up to 10 cubic feet in volume and no more than 40 Bulk to preserve it for 1 week. The object doesn't decay, and effects that require the object to be fresh don't count the time passing during this duration as having elapsed. When sprinkled on a corpse, this extends the period in which a creature can be revived by magic, as well as the wait time required before a corpse can be targeted again with [_talking corpse_](/Spells.aspx?ID=329). The salts prevent ordinary pests from consuming the target (such as maggots for a corpse or moths for a piece of clothing). Any creature can use an Interact action to disperse the salts from an unattended object and end this effect.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Timeless Salts

[Universal Solvent](/Equipment.aspx?ID=701)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Alchemical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=6" />
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Advanced Player's Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=39) pg. 255

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="Single Action" /><row>Interact</row></row>

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

Originally formulated as a means of dissolving sovereign glue, this powerful solvent can break almost any adhesive's grip. As universal solvent is particularly effective against [sovereign glue](/Equipment.aspx?ID=699), it automatically dissolves sovereign glue. It attempts to [counteract](/Rules.aspx?ID=371) any other adhesives, such as [_tanglefoot bags_](/Spells.aspx?ID=330), with a counteract modifier depending on the type.

</column>

**Source** [Advanced Player's Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=39) pg. 256 **Price** 900 gp --- You arrange a spiky cage of bones, particularly tough vegetation, or other material to spring up when disturbed. The snare deals 9d8 piercing damage to the first creature to enter this square; that creature must attempt a DC 31 Reflex save. **Critical Success** The creature is unaffected.
**Success** The creature is knocked aside by the snare's deployment, taking half damage, and is [flat-footed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=16) until the end of its next turn.
**Failure** The creature is captured by the cage, taking full damage and falling [prone](/Conditions.aspx?ID=31). It is [immobilized](/Conditions.aspx?ID=24) while it remains within the cage. It can get free by [Escaping](/Actions.aspx?ID=79) (DC 31) or by destroying the cage (Hardness 5, HP 30, object immunities).
**Critical Failure** As failure, but the creature takes double damage.
name: Engulfing Snare

[Flare Snare](/Equipment.aspx?ID=703)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />
<trait label="Mechanical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=105" />
<trait label="Snare" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=146" />

Using bioluminescent matter or alchemical reagents, you rig a short-lived flare to a trip wire or pressure plate. When a Small or larger creature enters the square, this flare shoots into the sky. To creatures with a clear view of the sky, this flare is visible from up to 2 miles away on a clear day or up to 5 miles away on a clear night.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Flare Snare

This snare snags a creature with its wicked metal hooks. The first creature to enter the square takes 5d8 piercing damage and 5d8 slashing damage, with a DC 27 basic Reflex save. On a critical failure, the hooks piercing its flesh make the creature [immobilized](/Conditions.aspx?ID=24) until it successfully [Escapes](/Actions.aspx?ID=79) (DC 27).

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

    level="1"
    right="Item 2"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Antler Arrow](/Equipment.aspx?ID=706)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Conjuration" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=33" />
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Advanced Player's Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=39) pg. 256

**Price** 7 gp

<row gap="medium">
**Ammunition** arrow

</row>

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="Single Action" /><row>Interact</row></row>

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

Creation of these arrows was inspired by an encounter with a [horned archon](/Monsters.aspx?ID=31) scout who sought to peacefully restrain their foes. When an activated _antler arrow_ hits a target, bone antlers extend to pin it down. The target must succeed at a DC 16 Reflex save or become stuck to the surface, taking the critical specialization effects of a [bow](/WeaponGroups.aspx?ID=3).

 If the hit with the _antler arrow_ is a critical hit and you have access to the bow critical specialization effect, the DC of the [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) check increases to 15.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Antler Arrow

    pfs="Standard"
>
[Balisse Feather](/Equipment.aspx?ID=707)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />
<trait label="Divine" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=48" />
<trait label="Evocation" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=65" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
<trait label="Talisman" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=155" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Advanced Player's Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=39) pg. 256

**Price** 400 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** affixed to a weapon

</row>

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="Free Action" /><row>envision</row></row>

 

**Trigger** You deal damage using the affixed weapon to a creature you have seen commit an evil act.

 
</row>

</column>

---

This long, fire-red feather seems to catch flame every time the weapon it adorns strikes a purveyor of evil. When you activate the feather, the creature you damaged burns with holy light. The creature must succeed at a DC 29 Will save or take a –2 status penalty to AC and saving throws and reduce its resistances by 5. These effects last until the end of your next turn.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Balisse Feather

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 16"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Candle of Invocation](/Equipment.aspx?ID=709)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Conjuration" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=33" />
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />
<trait label="Divine" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=48" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Advanced Player's Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=39) pg. 256

**Price** 2,000 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

</row>

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="Single Action" /><row>Interact</row></row>

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This golden candle bears the symbol of a specific deity emblazoned on its surface, surrounded by the iconography of that deity's faith. Once lit, this candle burns for 1 hour, and it can't be extinguished.

 Characters who cast divine spells and worship the deity emblazoned on the candle can perform their daily preparations within 10 feet of the lit candle to gain two additional spell slots for the day, each at half the highest spell slot they possess (for example, 4th-level slots if the caster can cast 8th-level spells). Prepared casters must prepare spells in these spell slots as they do for all their other spell slots.

 A caster's additional spell slots from the candle are lost the next time that caster performs their daily preparations. No one can benefit from more than one candle of invocation in a day, but multiple characters can benefit from a single candle's effects.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Candle of Invocation

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 7"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Candle of Revealing](/Equipment.aspx?ID=710)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />
<trait label="Divination" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=47" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Advanced Player's Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=39) pg. 257

**Price** 60 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

</row>

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="Single Action" /><row>Interact</row></row>

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

When lit, this black candle's eerie blue flame reveals the presence of invisible creatures. Within a 10-foot radius of the lit candle, creatures don't benefit from the [invisible](/Conditions.aspx?ID=26) condition. Their bodies are outlined, not fully visible, so they are [concealed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=4). Once lit, the candle burns for 1 minute, after which the effect ends. If extinguished, it can't be relit.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Candle of Revealing

>
[Corrosive Ammunition](/Equipment.aspx?ID=711)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />
<trait label="Evocation" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=65" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />

---

This peculiar ammunition is coated in yellow dust that leaves a stain on anything it touches. When activated _corrosive ammunition_ hits a target, it dissolves across the target's armor. The armor takes 1d8 [persistent acid damage](/Conditions.aspx?ID=29) that ignores the armor's Hardness; if the target isn't wearing armor, it takes the acid damage instead. This damage occurs at the end of the target's turns.

 The creature can end this effect by spending an Interact action to wipe off the corrosive dust, and otherwise the effect ends once the armor becomes [broken](/Conditions.aspx?ID=2).

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Corrosive Ammunition

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 0"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Dust of Corpse Animation](/Equipment.aspx?ID=712)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
<trait label="Necromancy" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=117" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Advanced Player's Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=39) pg. 257

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 2 hands

</row>

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="Two Actions" /><row>Interact</row></row>

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This black pouch contains what appears to be fine bone dust. Pouring the dust in a special pattern over a corpse turns it into an undead creature. The type of undead created depends on the condition of the corpse, resulting in either a [skeleton](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=92) or a [zombie](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=103). If the undead's level would be greater than 3, the dust fails to animate it. The body must be of an appropriate size and type for the undead you wish to create—for example, you must sprinkle the dust on a horse's skeleton to animate a [skeletal horse](/Monsters.aspx?ID=374). If more than one undead in the level range is appropriate, such as [skeletal guard](/Monsters.aspx?ID=372) or [skeletal champion](/Monsters.aspx?ID=373) for a Medium humanoid skeleton, you choose.

 The animated undead has the [minion](/Traits.aspx?ID=109) trait, meaning it can use 2 actions when you Command it. You can issue a Command for the current turn as part of the activation. The undead creature remains animated for 1 minute before collapsing back into its corpse form. As usual, you can have a maximum of four minions under your control.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Dust of Corpse Animation

<traits>

<trait label="Evocation" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=65" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />

</row>

</column>

---

This bright oil, first created by humans as a tool to help them fight in darkness, holds flecks of tiny gemstones in suspension and smells like a struck [tindertwig](/Equipment.aspx?ID=138).

 The first time a weapon coated with this oil damages a creature, the wound glows with light for 1 minute. If the creature is [invisible](/Conditions.aspx?ID=26), the light's position means it is merely [hidden](/Conditions.aspx?ID=22) to creatures that would otherwise be unable to see it, rather than [undetected](/Conditions.aspx?ID=39). The light also negates the [concealed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=4) condition due to lighting conditions. If the coated weapon doesn't damage a creature within 1 hour, the oil sloughs off and loses its power.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Oil of Revelation

This thin, black liquid imparts a bone-deep chill while rapidly repairing an [undead](/Traits.aspx?ID=160) creature's physical or spiritual form. When you dash _oil of unlife_ onto an undead creature, or a living creature with [negative healing](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=42), the oil absorbs quickly into its body, and the creature regains the listed number of Hit Points. You can pour _oil of unlife_ on an [incorporeal](/Traits.aspx?ID=222) undead; in this case, the creature absorbs the oil into itself.
