<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 0"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Potion of Disguise](/Equipment.aspx?ID=719)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
<trait label="Polymorph" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=127" />
<trait label="Potion" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=130" />
<trait label="Transmutation" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=157" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Advanced Player's Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=39) pg. 258

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="Single Action" /><row>Interact</row></row>

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

Upon imbibing this potion, you take on the appearance of a specific type of creature for 2d12 hours. The type of creature is determined when the potion is created. For example, you might have a _potion of elf disguise_ or _potion of frog disguise_. Drinking the potion doesn't impart the knowledge of how long the effect lasts; the GM rolls the duration in secret.

 The disguise doesn't change your traits or statistics, nor does it give you any of the special abilities of the creature you're imitating. It might affect what items you can hold or wear (for example, your new form might lack opposable thumbs). The potion shrinks you down to a minimum of size Small, or maintains your size if the creature is your size or larger. For example, if you are Medium and drink a _potion of fire giant disguise_, you'll take on the appearance of a [fire giant](/Monsters.aspx?ID=223) but remain Medium. The creature has to be of a specific kind, such as “[leopard](/Monsters.aspx?ID=67)” or “[lion](/Monsters.aspx?ID=68)” rather than just “cat,” or “fire giant” or “[ogre](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=78)” rather than just “giant,” but the potion can't cause you to mimic a specific individual creature.

 The effects of this potion use the same rules as the [Impersonate](/Actions.aspx?ID=46) activity of [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=5). Onlookers always assume you're the chosen type of creature unless they're actively [Seeking](/Actions.aspx?ID=84). You gain a +4 status bonus to your Deception DC against such Perception checks and add your level even if untrained.

When you drink this potion, you become [fleeing](/Conditions.aspx?ID=17) for 1 minute, and you gain a +40-foot status bonus to all your Speeds for the duration as long as you are fleeing. You immediately Stride.

A _potion of retaliation_ is available in four varieties—acid, cold, electricity, and fire—each with a faint shimmer reminiscent of the energy it contains. For 1 minute after drinking a _potion of retaliation_, you glow with a faint aura of that energy, and a creature that touches you (such as by making an unarmed attack or using a spell with a range of touch against you) takes damage of that type. The moderate, greater, and major versions also damage an adjacent creature that hits you with a melee weapon Strike.

</column>

<traits>
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />
<trait label="Divination" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=47" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />

---

A _potion of shared memories_ can transfer recollections from one creature to another. To place a memory in the potion, you must hold the vial and focus on a particular memory for 1 minute. This memory must be of a single event, location, person, or otherwise encompass a span of about 1 minute. The clear fluid takes on a shimmering hue reminiscent of the stored memory and gains a slightly sweet taste. 

Upon consuming the potion, the drinker vividly recalls the memory, and thereafter can remember it as easily as a memory they actually experienced. An unwilling drinker can refuse to absorb the memory.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Potion of Shared Memories

[Time Shield Potion](/Equipment.aspx?ID=726)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
<trait label="Potion" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=130" />
<trait label="Transmutation" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=157" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Advanced Player's Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=39) pg. 259

**Price** 600 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="Single Action" /><row>Interact</row></row>

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This purple potion has a bitter taste and seems to blur with motion. When you drink a _time shield potion_, you are frozen in time for 2d4 rounds. You can't act or be targeted, you become immune to all effects, and you vanish from your space; as far as the universe is concerned, you simply don't exist as long as the potion lasts. The durations of any effects targeting you when you drink the potion are suspended until it wears off. 

Once the potion's duration expires, you return to the normal flow of time and your former space; if that space is now occupied, you get shunted to the nearest unoccupied space. Effects with durations immediately resume affecting you, elapsing as though no time has passed. If you are within the area of an effect created while you were outside of time, you immediately take those effects upon returning. The GM might determine that other changes that occurred while you were outside of time (such as the ground beneath you crumbling) also affect you upon your return.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Time Shield Potion

This brown-and-gold robe covers you from head to toe. Its weighty fabric doesn't move with the wind, instead hanging still as if carved of stone.

**Activate** <actions string="Single Action" /> Interact;  **Frequency** once per hour;   **Effect** You [Burrow](/Actions.aspx?ID=93) through dirt and stone up to your land Speed, leaving no tunnels or signs of your passing. If you end your movement inside solid stone, you are forcibly expelled into the nearest open area, taking 1d6 bludgeoning damage for every 5 feet between the end of your movement and the open area.

<traits>

<trait label="Divination" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=47" />

This fine wooden box is inlaid with [Dwarven](/Languages.aspx?ID=3) runes, with hinges and a clasp of forged iron. The box contains a few handfuls of fine sand.

**Activate** <actions string="" /> 1 minute (envision, Interact);  **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** You hold the box closed and, while envisioning the earth around you, turn the box clockwise three times and then give it three slow shakes. When you open the box, the sand has arranged itself to replicate, in miniature, the stone terrain surrounding you, to a range of 60 feet. This shows details of paths; hills; embankments; boulders; and even artificial structures like walls, ditches, and tunnels, as long as they're made of stone and earth. If you're underground, it reveals tunnels and voids in the earth within 60 feet at your current depth. The sand maintains its shape until you close the box.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Earthsight Box

These strange masks consist of a thin stone hood and a shimmering purple veil reminiscent of the [pleroma aeon](/Monsters.aspx?ID=11) who first gifted them to mortals in the hope that sharing the means of aeon communication would reduce the misunderstandings and conflicts among mortal civilizations. An _envisioning mask_ covers your entire face, though it doesn't hinder your vision or other senses.

**Activate** <actions string="Single Action" /> Interact;  **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** You lose the ability to produce language and instead communicate wordlessly through a series of psychic projections. This acts as [telepathy](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=37) with a range of 100 feet, but it is understandable to all creatures regardless of whether they have a language. When communicating with non-[aeons](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=2), however, your meaning is often vague and mysterious. This effect lasts for 10 minutes.

</column>

    level="1"
    right="Item 10"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Fire-Jump Ring](/Equipment.aspx?ID=732)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Conjuration" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=33" />
<trait label="Fire" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=72" />
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=98" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
<trait label="Teleportation" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=156" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Advanced Player's Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=39) pg. 260

**Price** 940 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** worn

</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This dark-red ring is engraved with ashen symbols and smells faintly of smoke.

**Activate** <actions string="Two Actions" /> envision, Interact;  **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** You Stride (or [Burrow](/Actions.aspx?ID=93) or [Fly](/Actions.aspx?ID=94), if you have the corresponding Speed) into any fire large enough to contain you, including magical fires. You vanish into the fire and take no damage from it. You can sense all sufficiently large fires within 100 feet of where you vanish, and you reemerge from any of those fires, either within the fire or adjacent to it. If you end your movement in the fire, it affects you as normal.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Fire-Jump Ring

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 12"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Four-Ways Dogslicer](/Equipment.aspx?ID=733)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Cold" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=27" />
<trait label="Electricity" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=56" />
<trait label="Evocation" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=65" />
<trait label="Fire" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=72" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Advanced Player's Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=39) pg. 261

**Price** 1,700 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">
**Base Weapon** [Dogslicer](/Weapons.aspx?ID=49)
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This _+2 [striking](/Equipment.aspx?ID=280) [dogslicer](/Weapons.aspx?ID=49)_ is obviously the work of a brilliant but scrappy artisan who valued versatility over safety. Four toothy gems line the blade's cutting edge, three of which glow—one with fiery red light, one with a chill blue, and one that gives off sparks—while the last is a glistening black. Each of these gems embodies a weapon property rune, but only one rune can be active at a time.

**Activate** <actions string="Single Action" /> Interact;     **Effect** You twist the gem along the blade corresponding with the desired weapon rune: red for [_flaming_](/Equipment.aspx?ID=295), blue for [_frost_](/Equipment.aspx?ID=296), or yellow for [_shock_](/Equipment.aspx?ID=303). You take 1d6 damage of the type the chosen rune deals. You can instead twist the black gem to disable the active property rune, taking no damage.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Four-Ways Dogslicer

These heavy boots are made of blackened metal and always feel warm to the touch, with streams of glowing embers cascading off their heels. While wearing _hellfire boots_, you gain resistance 10 to fire damage.

**Activate** <actions string="Two Actions" /> Interact;  **Frequency** once per minute;   **Effect** Stride. Each square you move through during your Stride is scorched with hellish flames, becoming hazardous terrain for 1 minute. A creature that moves through one of these spaces takes 3d6 fire damage.

[Infiltrator's Accessory](/Equipment.aspx?ID=736)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Illusion" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=92" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Advanced Player's Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=39) pg. 261

**Price** 150 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

</row>

</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This elegant _+1 [striking](/Equipment.aspx?ID=280) [sword cane](/Weapons.aspx?ID=127)_ serves equally well as a fashionable accessory and hidden weapon suitable for high-society events where weapons aren't typically permitted.

**Activate** <actions string="Single Action" /> Interact;     **Effect** You twist the sword cane's hilt, magically merging the blade into the cane. While the blade is merged, it can't be drawn and gains the benefits of a 3rd-level [_magic aura_](/Spells.aspx?ID=178) spell to appear non-magical. Passive observers can't attempt a check to notice the cane is anything more than a mundane, if superb, fashion accessory. Those carefully examining it can discern the cane is more than it appears, but doing so is extremely difficult (DC 30 Perception). Activating the sword cane again releases the blade, allowing it to be drawn normally.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Infiltrator's Accessory

<traits>

<trait label="Enchantment" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=61" />

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** worn gloves

</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

---

These white silk gloves are adorned in red hearts that glow faintly whenever you are adjacent to someone you feel particularly strongly toward. They buoy your spirit, giving you a +1 item bonus to [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=6) checks.

**Activate** <actions string="Single Action" /> Interact;  **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** You grasp the hands of a willing creature you have strong positive feelings about, regardless of the nature of those feelings. The creature gains a +1 status bonus to saving throws and 10 temporary Hit Points for 10 minutes. If the creature shares your feelings, you gain the same benefits, and for the duration, when you both roll a success on a saving throw against an [emotion](/Traits.aspx?ID=60) effect that causes negative emotions, you both get a critical success instead.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Lover's Gloves

---

This ice-blue half-mask is adorned with a wicked silver grin that covers the wearer's mouth, leaving the rest of the face uncovered. You gain a +2 item bonus to [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=7) checks.

**Activate** <actions string="Two Actions" /> envision, Interact;  **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** The mask emits a soul-chilling scream that deals 6d10 negative damage to each living creature in a 20-foot emanation (DC 25 basic Fortitude save).

**Craft Requirements** Supply a casting of [_wail of the banshee_](/Spells.aspx?ID=361).

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 0"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Medusa's Scream](/Equipment.aspx?ID=739)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
<trait label="Transmutation" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=157" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Advanced Player's Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=39) pg. 261

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

**Bulk** 1
</row>

<row gap="medium">
**Base Shield** [Steel Shield](/Shields.aspx?ID=3)
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

The ghastly visage of a slain [medusa's](/Monsters.aspx?ID=297) head stares out from this [steel shield](/Shields.aspx?ID=3) (Hardness 13, HP 52, BT 26). The shield comes with a thick leather cover to conceal the head.

**Activate** <actions string="Two Actions" /> Interact ([visual](/Traits.aspx?ID=163)) **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** You reveal the medusa's face, focusing its gaze on one creature within 30 feet. The shield casts [_flesh to stone_](/Spells.aspx?ID=123) with a range of 30 feet.

**Craft Requirements** The initial raw materials must include the head of a [medusa](/Monsters.aspx?ID=297).

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Medusa's Scream

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 1"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Predictable Silver Piece](/Equipment.aspx?ID=740)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Divination" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=47" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Advanced Player's Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=39) pg. 262

**Price** 3 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This seemingly unremarkable, weathered silver coin bears the bust of an unnamed monarch on the face and a majestic bird on the tail. You can toss the coin without activating it, in which case it follows the normal laws of probability.

**Activate** <actions string="Single Action" /> Interact;     **Effect** You rub your thumb on one side of the coin with the intent of slightly tweaking the strands of fate, then flip the coin into the air in a coin toss. No matter how the toss is resolved—letting the coin fall to the ground, slapping it down on the back of your hand, or catching it on your open palm—it always lands with the side you rubbed face up.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Predictable Silver Piece

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 20"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Rod of Cancellation](/Equipment.aspx?ID=742)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Rare" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=137" />
<trait label="Abjuration" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=2" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Advanced Player's Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=39) pg. 262

**Price** 75,000 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

**Bulk** 1
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This powerful magic rod is inimical to all magic.

**Activate** <actions string="Two Actions" /> Interact;     **Effect** You touch the rod to a magical effect or magic item and attempt to [counteract](/Rules.aspx?ID=371) the effect or item. Regardless of the result, the _rod of cancellation_ can't be activated again for 2d6 hours. On a success, the effect or item is deactivated for the same amount of time, and its duration, if any, continues to expire during that time. If you choose, you can instead completely drain the _rod of cancellation's_ magic on a success in order to completely drain the magic from the effect or item. If you do, both become completely non-magical and their magic can't be recovered, even by the [_remake_](/Spells.aspx?ID=249) spell.

 The _rod of cancellation_ automatically fails to counteract most [artifacts](/Equipment.aspx?Category=45) and similarly powerful items, but it reacts explosively when activated against a [_sphere of annihilation_](/Equipment.aspx?ID=620) without a counteract check.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Rod of Cancellation

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 3+"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Rope of Climbing](/Equipment.aspx?ID=743)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
<trait label="Transmutation" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=157" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Advanced Player's Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=39) pg. 262

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This silk rope measures 50 feet long and is capable of holding up to 3,000 pounds. If the rope is ever cut, only the longest remaining portion retains its magic.

**Activate** <actions string="Single Action" /> Interact;  **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** You hold one end of the rope and point to a destination. The rope animates for 1 minute, moving 10 feet per round until it reaches the destination or runs out of length. The rope can move across any non-damaging horizontal or vertical surface, but it can't extend upward without a surface to support it. At any point while the rope is animated, you can use an Interact action to wiggle the rope, giving it one of the following commands: stop in place, fasten securely to the nearest available object, detach from an object, or knot or unknot itself.

</column>

    level="1"
    right="Item 13"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Slates of Distant Letters](/Equipment.aspx?ID=744)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Divination" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=47" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Advanced Player's Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=39) pg. 262

**Price** 2,450 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 2 hands

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This matched pair of slates, roughly one handspan wide and tall, have identical ornate frames. Slates are crafted in pairs, and each works with only the other of its pair. If one slate of a pair is ever broken, the other shatters into non-magical shards. The listed price is for a pair of slates.

**Activate** <actions string="Two Actions" /> Interact;  **Frequency** once per hour;   **Effect** You use a piece of chalk to write up to 25 words on a slate. As you write, the writing also appears on the other slate in its matched pair, no matter how far away it is, as long as it is on the same plane. Wiping one slate clean erases the writing from both slates. Each slate can be activated once per hour. 

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Slates of Distant Letters

markdown:
<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 0"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Sleeves of Storage](/Equipment.aspx?ID=745)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Conjuration" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=33" />
<trait label="Extradimensional" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=67" />
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=98" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Advanced Player's Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=39) pg. 263

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** worn garment

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This loose robe has wide, voluminous sleeves that each contain an extradimensional space. These spaces each function as a [_bag of holding_](/Equipment.aspx?ID=249) that can hold up to 5 Bulk of items (for a total of 10 Bulk), though no individual item can be of more than 1 Bulk; the sleeves grow slightly heavy as you reach maximum capacity. You can add or remove an item from a sleeve with a single hand free as an Interact action.

 If a sleeve is completely empty, you can place your own familiar into that extradimensional space. It can survive comfortably in your sleeve for up to 1 hour, after which it begins to [suffocate](/Rules.aspx?ID=468). While in your sleeve, it can't be affected or targeted by any effects, but you don't benefit from any master abilities. A familiar can exit the sleeve of its own volition with a single action that has the [manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=104) and [move](/Traits.aspx?ID=114) traits. You can't place any other creature into your sleeves, nor can you place your familiar in a sleeve if it's larger than Tiny. If your familiar is in your sleeve, you can't place any items in the sleeve.

<column gap="tiny">

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 0"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Staff of Impossible Visions](/Equipment.aspx?ID=748)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Illusion" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=92" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
<trait label="Staff" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=151" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Advanced Player's Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=39) pg. 263

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

**Bulk** 1
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This bizarre staff is made from oak, capped with a cluster of eye-shaped gemstones that seem to move and undulate at the corner of your vision. While wielding the staff, you can peer through the eyes, using your normal visual senses (including any benefits of spells affecting your vision, like [_see invisibility_](/Spells.aspx?ID=271)). You can maneuver the staff to see things around corners, at higher elevations, or in places where the staff can fit but your head can't. This doesn't provide sufficient line of effect to target creatures around corners. The eyes are as vulnerable as your eyes and can be affected by anything that alters your vision, such as a blinding flash of light.

**Activate** <actions string="" /> Cast a Spell;     **Effect** You expend a number of charges from the staff to cast a spell from its list.

**Craft Requirements** Supply one casting of all listed levels of all listed spells.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Staff of Impossible Visions

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 9"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Urn of Ashes](/Equipment.aspx?ID=751)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
<trait label="Necromancy" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=117" />
<trait label="Negative" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=118" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Advanced Player's Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=39) pg. 264

**Price** 700 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This sealed pewter urn contains the ashes of a benevolent ancestor, with a sliver of lingering spirit that strives to protect you.

**Activate** <actions string="Reaction" /> envision;   **Trigger** You would become [doomed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=9) or your doomed value would increase; **Requirements** The ashes aren't doomed; **Effect** The ashes in the urn intervene, taking the doomed condition in your place, and you don't gain or increase the value of your own doomed condition. Each night when you get a full night's rest, you can reduce your own doomed condition or that of the urn, but not both.

**Activate** <actions string="Single Action" /> envision, Interact ([attack](/Traits.aspx?ID=15)) **Frequency** once per round;   **Effect** The urn shoots a bolt of negative energy at a foe within 30 feet. Attempt a spell attack roll against the target's AC, using a modifier of +15 or your own spell attack roll modifier, whichever is higher. On a success, the bolt deals 4d4 negative damage (doubled on a critical success).

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Urn of Ashes

markdown:
**Source** [Advanced Player's Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=39) pg. 264 **Usage** worn armor **Bulk** 4 **Base Armor** [Full Plate](/Armor.aspx?ID=13) --- The metal plates of this _+1 [resilient](/Equipment.aspx?ID=279) [full plate](/Armor.aspx?ID=13)_ are covered by a bright tabard featuring a distinctive coat of arms divided into four fields. This insignia magically records your recent victories, displaying one triumph in each field of the coat of arms, allowing you to call upon those victories for aid in future battles. **Activate** envision; **Trigger** You kill or destroy a creature of a level equal to or greater than your _victory plate_ (adjusted as usual if you add fundamental runes to _victory plate_). This creature must have one of the traits listed in the second activation; **Effect** You direct the armor to commemorate your victory. The coat of arms on the armor's tabard shifts to incorporate heraldry related to the slain creature, and the victory is recorded within the tabard. If you have already recorded four victories, choose one to replace with the new victory. **Activate** envision, Interact; **Frequency** once per hour; **Effect** You touch one of the tabard's four fields representing one of your victories and recall your triumph over that creature. That creature vanishes from the tabard, and for 1 minute, you gain resistance 5 to a damage type based on the creature's trait (you gain resistance to only one type, even if the creature has more than one trait that could apply):
  • [Aberration](/Traits.aspx?ID=1) or [Fey](/Traits.aspx?ID=69) mental
  • [Celestial](/Traits.aspx?ID=23) good
  • [Construct](/Traits.aspx?ID=35) poison
  • [Dragon](/Traits.aspx?ID=50) or [Elemental](/Traits.aspx?ID=57) one energy type that matches one of the dragon's or elemental's traits
  • [Fiend](/Traits.aspx?ID=70) evil
  • [Ooze](/Traits.aspx?ID=122) precision, and the resistance is 10
  • [Undead](/Traits.aspx?ID=160) negative

markdown:
**Source** [Advanced Player's Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=39) pg. 264 **Usage** worn armor **Bulk** 4 **Base Armor** [Full Plate](/Armor.aspx?ID=13) --- The metal plates of this _+1 [resilient](/Equipment.aspx?ID=279) [full plate](/Armor.aspx?ID=13)_ are covered by a bright tabard featuring a distinctive coat of arms divided into four fields. This insignia magically records your recent victories, displaying one triumph in each field of the coat of arms, allowing you to call upon those victories for aid in future battles. **Activate** envision; **Trigger** You kill or destroy a creature of a level equal to or greater than your _victory plate_ (adjusted as usual if you add fundamental runes to _victory plate_). This creature must have one of the traits listed in the second activation; **Effect** You direct the armor to commemorate your victory. The coat of arms on the armor's tabard shifts to incorporate heraldry related to the slain creature, and the victory is recorded within the tabard. If you have already recorded four victories, choose one to replace with the new victory. **Activate** envision, Interact; **Frequency** once per hour; **Effect** You touch one of the tabard's four fields representing one of your victories and recall your triumph over that creature. That creature vanishes from the tabard, and for 1 minute, you gain resistance 5 to a damage type based on the creature's trait (you gain resistance to only one type, even if the creature has more than one trait that could apply):
  • [Aberration](/Traits.aspx?ID=1) or [Fey](/Traits.aspx?ID=69) mental
  • [Celestial](/Traits.aspx?ID=23) good
  • [Construct](/Traits.aspx?ID=35) poison
  • [Dragon](/Traits.aspx?ID=50) or [Elemental](/Traits.aspx?ID=57) one energy type that matches one of the dragon's or elemental's traits
  • [Fiend](/Traits.aspx?ID=70) evil
  • [Ooze](/Traits.aspx?ID=122) precision, and the resistance is 10
  • [Undead](/Traits.aspx?ID=160) negative
Victory Plate **Source** [Advanced Player's Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=39) pg. 264 **Price** 675 gp **Bulk** 4 Victory Plate (Greater) **Source** [Advanced Player's Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=39) pg. 264 **Price** 5,500 gp **Bulk** 4 --- The armor is a _+2 greater resilient full plate_. You can use the second activation once per minute instead of once per hour, and the resistance it grants is 10, or 20 for the ooze's resistance against precision damage.
name: Victory Plate

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 1"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Walking Cauldron](/Equipment.aspx?ID=753)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=98" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
<trait label="Transmutation" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=157" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Advanced Player's Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=39) pg. 264

**Price** 12 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Bulk** 4
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This iron cauldron stands upon sturdy iron crow's feet. A _walking cauldron_ has a land Speed of 25 feet and can be used as a suitable tool to [Craft](/Actions.aspx?ID=43) potions, oils, or other liquids.

 As a single action, which has the [auditory](/Traits.aspx?ID=16) and [concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=32) traits, you can command the cauldron to either follow you or to stand in place. When following you, the cauldron does its best to remain within 30 feet of you, but its ungainly movements are too imprecise to predictably direct in a combat encounter or other situation where seconds and precise locations count. It can carry up to 2 Bulk of ingredients for potions or other liquids inside of itself while following you, but if overloaded or if you put anything else inside it, it stands in place and refuses to move until 10 minutes after you remove the excess.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Walking Cauldron

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 8+"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Wand of Crackling Lightning](/Equipment.aspx?ID=754)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Electricity" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=56" />
<trait label="Evocation" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=65" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
<trait label="Wand" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=164" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Advanced Player's Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=39) pg. 265

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This wand is made of two copper plates separated by a ceramic center.

**Activate** <actions string="" /> Cast a Spell;  **Frequency** once per day, plus overcharge;   **Effect** You cast [_lightning bolt_](/Spells.aspx?ID=172), but the spell's area is twice as wide (two adjacent and parallel 120-foot lines) and creatures that fail their save are [flat-footed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=16) for 1 round.

**Craft Requirements** Supply a casting of _lightning bolt_ of the appropriate level.

<traits>

<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
<trait label="Necromancy" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=117" />
<trait label="Positive" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=128" />
<trait label="Wand" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=164" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Advanced Player's Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=39) pg. 265

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This alabaster wand has a clear crystal at the tip.

**Activate** <actions string="" /> Cast a Spell;  **Frequency** once per day, plus overcharge;   **Effect** You cast [_heal_](/Spells.aspx?ID=148) at the indicated level. After you cast the spell, at the start of your next turn, excess healing magic wells up from the wand and heals you, as though you cast the 1-action version of _heal_ on yourself at the same spell level. You gain this benefit only once, even if you cast multiple _heal_ spells from _wands of overflowing life_ in the same turn.

**Craft Requirements** Supply a casting of _heal_ of the appropriate level.

**Activate** <actions string="" /> Cast a Spell;  **Frequency** once per day, plus overcharge;   **Effect** You cast [_heal_](/Spells.aspx?ID=148) at the indicated level. After you cast the spell, at the start of your next turn, excess healing magic wells up from the wand and heals you, as though you cast the 1-action version of _heal_ on yourself at the same spell level. You gain this benefit only once, even if you cast multiple _heal_ spells from _wands of overflowing life_ in the same turn.

**Craft Requirements** Supply a casting of _heal_ of the appropriate level.

    level="1"
    right="Item 0"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Winged](/Equipment.aspx?ID=759)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
<trait label="Transmutation" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=157" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Advanced Player's Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=39) pg. 265

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** etched onto armor

</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This rune is a swirling glyph on the front of the armor. A large pair of transparent, ephemeral wings floats out from the back of the armor.

**Activate** <actions string="Two Actions" /> command, Interact;  **Frequency** once per hour;   **Effect** You trace the rune on the front of the breastplate and the armor's ephemeral wings grow tangible, granting you a fly Speed of 25 feet or your land Speed, whichever is slower. This effect lasts for 5 minutes or until you Dismiss it. Once the effect ends, the wings disappear completely, reappearing in their ephemeral form 1 hour later.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Winged

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 11"
    pfs="Limited"
>
[Fiendish Teleportation](/Equipment.aspx?ID=760)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Rare" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=137" />
<trait label="Conjuration" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=33" />
<trait label="Contract" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=329" />
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=98" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Legends](/Sources.aspx?ID=40) pg. 10

<row gap="medium">

</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

You tap into the fiendish ability to slip through space. When you Stride, you gain a +3 item bonus to Armor Class against reactions triggered by your movement. Once per day, from any distance, Abrogail Thrune II can call on a provision in your Thrune contract as a single action, causing you to become [paralyzed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=28) for 1 hour or until Abrogail releases you, whichever comes first.

**Activate** <actions string="Two Actions" /> command;  **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** You recite a subclause of your contract regarding change in venue. You cast [_dimension door_](/Spells.aspx?ID=69). The space you leave and the one you appear in are filled with the scent of brimstone, dealing 2d6 evil damage to creatures adjacent to both spaces.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Fiendish Teleportation

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 15"
    pfs="Restricted"
>
[Infernal Health](/Equipment.aspx?ID=761)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Rare" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=137" />
<trait label="Contract" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=329" />
<trait label="Healing" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=89" />
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=98" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Legends](/Sources.aspx?ID=40) pg. 10

<row gap="medium">

</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

You regain triple the normal number of Hit Points when resting (meaning you regain triple your Constitution modifier multiplied by your level). The healing you gain from long-term rest is similarly tripled. Once per day, from any distance, Abrogail Thrune II can recite a voidability clause in your Thrune contract as a 1-minute activity to prevent you from regaining Hit Points from resting for 1 day.

**Activate** <actions string="Two Actions" /> command;  **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** You recite a subclause regarding mandatory remediation from your Thrune contract. For 6 rounds, at the start of your turn each round, you recover 25 Hit Points unless you took good damage since the start of your previous turn.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Infernal Health

    level="1"
    right="Item 20"
    pfs="Restricted"
>
[Unending Youth](/Equipment.aspx?ID=762)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Rare" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=137" />
<trait label="Conjuration" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=33" />
<trait label="Contract" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=329" />
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=98" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />

<row gap="medium">

</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

You cease aging, and if you were older than young adult in age, you become a young adult again. You gain a +4 status bonus to saves against [death](/Traits.aspx?ID=40) effects and resistance to negative damage equal to half your level. Once per day, from any distance, Abrogail Thrune II can recite a mandatory appearance clause in your Thrune contract as a 3-action activity to summon you to her side, where you are [controlled](/Conditions.aspx?ID=6) by Abrogail for 1 minute before you return.

**Activate** <actions string="Two Actions" /> command;  **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** You recite a hold harmless provision from your Thrune contract. Reduce your [doomed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=9) value to 0. Abrogail Thrune II is immediately made aware that you have used this ability.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Unending Youth

    right="Item 18"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Sun Orchid Poultice](/Equipment.aspx?ID=763)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Rare" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=137" />
<trait label="Alchemical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=6" />
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Legends](/Sources.aspx?ID=40) pg. 20

**Price** 5,000 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="Single Action" /><row>Interact</row></row>

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

The process of creating the [sun orchid elixir](/Equipment.aspx?ID=497) leaves behind a large amount of thick paste with its own healing properties. Artokus commonly sells this paste to Thuvian natives who can't purchase the elixir proper and uses the income to support his alchemy students. When you apply the sun orchid poultice, it reduces your [clumsy](/Conditions.aspx?ID=3), [drained](/Conditions.aspx?ID=10), and [enfeebled](/Conditions.aspx?ID=13) condition values by 2. In addition, the poultice provides a youthful burst of energy, granting you a +3 item bonus to saves and 20 temporary Hit Points for 1 hour.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Sun Orchid Poultice

[Clockwork Recorder](/Equipment.aspx?ID=764)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Rare" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=137" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Legends](/Sources.aspx?ID=40) pg. 24

_[**PFS Note**](/pathfinder-society) Spare wax cylinders for a clockwork recorder cost 3 gp each and are the same rarity as the recorder itself (rare)._

**Price** 250 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This small recording device can be as tiny as a music box or around the size of large book and is typically concealed in hollowed-out books and jewelry. A clockwork recorder can record up to 1 hour of sound before its wax cylinders must be retrieved and replaced. Any given clockwork recorder can play back the recordings of a cylinder, regardless of whether it was the recorder used for the original recording.

**Activate** <actions string="Three Actions" /> Interact;     **Effect** You wind the recorder to start recording sound or to play back a recording. You can have the recording or playback start immediately or be timed to start at any point up to one month later.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Clockwork Recorder

    right="Item 2"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Lovers' Ink](/Equipment.aspx?ID=765)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Rare" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=137" />
<trait label="Alchemical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=6" />
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Legends](/Sources.aspx?ID=40) pg. 24

**Price** 5 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** with  writing set 

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This ink, derived from inks used by lovers to deliver secret messages, dries to a color similar to most parchments. Avarneus's version requires another dose of lover's ink and a specific agitation to reveal the hidden message, preventing casual interception. Any message written with lovers' ink is revealed by applying a page worth of lover's ink and vigorously shaking, requiring 3 Interact actions. A typical vial provides enough ink to fill 1 page worth of text. While the text is hidden, a creature closely examining a surface marked with lover's ink can detect the presence of the ink with a successful DC 25 Perception check. On a critical success, they can make out the ink well enough to use [Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=14) to [Decipher Writing](/Actions.aspx?ID=22).

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Lovers' Ink

>
[Palm Crossbow](/Equipment.aspx?ID=766)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Rare" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=137" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Legends](/Sources.aspx?ID=40) pg. 25

_[**PFS Note**](/pathfinder-society) Spare bolts for a palm crossbow cost 2 sp for 10 bolts and are the same rarity as the crossbow itself (rare)._

**Price** 60 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** worn

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This thick, elegant bracelet conceals a specialized firing mechanism that can hold a single blowgun dart. You can fire the dart normally from the bracelet. Recognizing the bracelet's nature requires a successful DC 25 Perception check.

**Activate** <actions string="Two Actions" /> Interact;     **Effect** You expand the bracelet into a [hand crossbow](/Weapons.aspx?ID=69). The bracelet has enough pieces to assemble up to three bolts, but the bolts contain necessary components for the bracelet. Without all of the bolt pieces, you cannot collapse the crossbow back into a bracelet.

<column gap="tiny">

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 3"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Vestige Lenses](/Equipment.aspx?ID=767)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Rare" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=137" />
<trait label="Alchemical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=6" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Legends](/Sources.aspx?ID=40) pg. 25

**Price** 40 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** worn eyepiece

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

These simple lenses are alchemically treated to detect the faint smoke trail from a piece of specially formulated sandalwood incense. This incense has an odor undetectable by most people and a long burn time, making it perfect for discreetly tracking individuals. A stick of this incense costs 1 sp and can safely burn for up to 8 hours. While wearing the lenses, you see alchemical fumes in a distinct green tint, granting you a +1 item bonus to [Survival](/Skills.aspx?ID=16) checks to [Track](/Actions.aspx?ID=66) a creature marked with the incense and to Perception checks to [Seek](/Actions.aspx?ID=84) any alchemical vapors. If the smoke is fresh, Tracking via the incense's fumes might use a lower DC than normal for tracking a creature walking on firm surfaces.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Vestige Lenses

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 14"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Deepdread Claw](/Equipment.aspx?ID=768)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Rare" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=137" />
<trait label="Evocation" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=65" />
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=98" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Legends](/Sources.aspx?ID=40) pg. 28

_[**PFS Note**](/pathfinder-society) For the purposes of Society play, the telepathic link created by a deepdread claw (page 28) ends when a Society adventure ends._

**Price** 4,000 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 or 2 hands

**Bulk** 1
</row>

<row gap="medium">
**Base Weapon** [Spear](/Weapons.aspx?ID=10)
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

One of a set of four identical spears collectively known as the Four Claws of the Deepdread, this weapon is fashioned from a single seamless piece of matte-black metal with a razored silver edge. In bright light, it functions as a _+1 [striking](/Equipment.aspx?ID=280) [spear](/Weapons.aspx?ID=10)_, but in darkness or dim light, it becomes a _+2 greater striking spear_. You can upgrade its fundamental runes as normal for a specific weapon, starting from a _+2 greater striking spear_, but its fundamental runes are always one type worse in bright light.

**Activate** <actions string="Single Action" /> command;     **Effect** You establish a telepathic link with someone else wielding a _deepdread claw_, enabling you to telepathically communicate with the creature while they possess the _deepdread claw_ regardless of distance, so long as you remain on the same plane. You can end a telepathic link you create or that you are part of as an action, which has the [concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=32) trait.

**Activate** <actions string="Two Actions" /> command, Interact;     **Effect** You cast 4th-level [_darkness_](/Spells.aspx?ID=59). You can see clearly in this darkness.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Deepdread Claw

---

Among Hao Jin's most precious treasures are three living plants, the last surviving celestial peach trees taken from the mountains of Chu Ye. One of the trees grows pearls in place of flowers, but the other two bear fruit that is far more valuable. Eating one of these small red peaches can heal even the most grievous of injuries.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

<title
level="2"
right="Item 17"
pfs="Standard"
>
Celestial Peach (Rejuvenation)

</traits>

<row gap="medium">

---

You gain the effects of 7th-level [_neutralize poison_](/Spells.aspx?ID=207), [_regenerate_](/Spells.aspx?ID=248), [_remove curse_](/Spells.aspx?ID=250), and [_remove disease_](/Spells.aspx?ID=251) spells. The peach has a [counteract](/Rules.aspx?ID=371) modifier of +27.

right="Item 20"
pfs="Standard"
>

<row gap="medium">

**Bulk** L

</row>

</column>

---

When you place the peach into the mouth of an intact corpse that died within the last year, it casts a 10th-level [_raise dead_](/Spells.aspx?ID=243) on the corpse.

    level="1"
    right="Item 16"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Mountain to the Sky](/Equipment.aspx?ID=770)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Unique" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=161" />
<trait label="Conjuration" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=33" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
<trait label="Structure" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=153" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Legends](/Sources.aspx?ID=40) pg. 48

**Price** 10,000 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

</row>

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><row>1 minute (Interact)</row></row>

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This tiny, carved walnut shell contains a sacred mountain within. You can only activate _mountain to the sky_ on an unoccupied patch of earth or soil. When activated, the walnut transforms into an impossibly steep mountain, 5,000 feet tall and 100 feet wide at the base. Climbing the mountain requires 8 hours and a successful DC 35 [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) check. After activating it, if you climb to the top of the mountain without any assistance from flight or magic, the mountain [_plane shifts_](/Spells.aspx?ID=222) you to [Heaven](/Planes.aspx?ID=17) when you reach the summit. You can return the _mountain to the sky_ into the shape of a walnut shell as a 1-minute activity, which has the [concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=32) and [manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=104) traits, so long as no living creatures are present on the mountain.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Mountain to the Sky

[Ruby String](/Equipment.aspx?ID=771)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Rare" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=137" />
<trait label="Conjuration" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=33" />
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />

**Price** 900 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

The frayed threads from the rips and tears in _Hao Jin's Tapestry_ still contain traces of the Ruby Phoenix's magic. Hao Jin has collected most of these loose strings, but others occasionally find these potent silk strands in unexpected places. A ruby string can fulfill the cost requirement for an 8th-level [_create demiplane_](/Rituals.aspx?ID=31) ritual. When used in this way, the ruby string negates the need for secondary casters, and you get a success for all secondary checks. Alternatively, you can activate it to create a smaller demiplane without requiring the ritual.

**Activate** <actions string="" /> 8 hours (envision, Interact);     **Effect** You spin the thread to form a demiplane with the effects of a successful 8th-level _create demiplane_, but the space is a single 10-foot cube.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Ruby String

<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="Single Action" /><row>Interact</row></row>

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

The metal of these arrows is said to have come from a star that ventured too close to Golarion and was shot down by a moonlit archer. When you activate and shoot a _starshot arrow_, you take no range penalties against any target that you can personally detect. There must be a line of effect between you and the target.

</column>

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 0"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Vaccine](/Equipment.aspx?ID=773)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Rare" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=137" />
<trait label="Alchemical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=6" />
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />
<trait label="Elixir" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=59" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Legends](/Sources.aspx?ID=40) pg. 71, [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 61

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="Single Action" /><row>Interact</row></row>

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

A vaccine grants a creature immunity to a specific strain of [disease](/Traits.aspx?ID=46) of a level equal to or less than the vaccine's level, and a +2 item bonus on all saving throws against other strains of the same disease. For example, a vaccine could grant immunity to filth fever inflicted by [otyughs](/Monsters.aspx?ID=327), but would only grant a +2 saving throw bonus against filth fever inflicted by a [giant rat](/Monsters.aspx?ID=346).

**Craft Requirements** Creating a vaccine requires a sample of the disease in question.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Vaccine

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 1+"
    pfs="Restricted"
>
[Addiction Suppressant](/Equipment.aspx?ID=774)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Rare" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=137" />
<trait label="Alchemical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=6" />
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />
<trait label="Elixir" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=59" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Legends](/Sources.aspx?ID=40) pg. 72

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="Single Action" /><row>Interact</row></row>

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

When you use a dose of addiction suppressant, it suppresses the effects of that addiction for 1 day, as if you had taken an actual dose of the [drug](/Traits.aspx?ID=261), but without any of the drug's effects, and it doesn't increase the addiction DC. You also gain an item bonus against the ongoing save against the drug's addiction depending on the type of addiction suppressant.

For every drug, a specific suppressant must be crafted— no generalized addiction suppressant yet exists. In order to create an addiction suppressant, you must begin with a dose of the drug to be suppressed, then use alchemical reagents to generate an elixir that can be used to fight that drug's addiction. An addiction suppressant must be a higher level than the drug it is meant to suppress in order to work.

</column>

markdown:
<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 15"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Blade of the Black Sovereign](/Equipment.aspx?ID=775)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Unique" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=161" />
<trait label="Electricity" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=56" />
<trait label="Evocation" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=65" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Legends](/Sources.aspx?ID=40) pg. 76

**Price** 6,500 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 2 hands

**Bulk** 2
</row>

<row gap="medium">
**Base Weapon** [Greatsword](/Weapons.aspx?ID=24)
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This _+2 [greater striking](/Equipment.aspx?ID=280) [shock](/Equipment.aspx?ID=303) [greatsword](/Weapons.aspx?ID=24)_ is forged from [standard-grade adamantine](/Equipment.aspx?ID=374), with inlays of broken circuitry and a robot's ocular lens on the cross guard. A soft thrum comes from the blade, which you feel as a subtle pulse through the grip when wielding it. It sparks when unsheathed, emitting dim light within 5 feet. On a hit against a foe made of metal, wearing metal armor, or using a metal shield, if the foe takes electricity damage from the blade, the makes the foe [flat-footed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=16) for 1 round; if the Strike was a critical hit, it also makes them [clumsy 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=3) for 1 round.

**Activate** <actions string="Two Actions" /> command, Interact;     **Effect** You cast the [_electric arc_](/Spells.aspx?ID=97) cantrip from the sword as an 8th-level primal spell, using 10 + your melee attack modifier with the _Blade of the Black Sovereign_ as your spell DC.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Blade of the Black Sovereign
